Stretched taut against the material of her suit, Garrus could even hear it groan as it digested the enormous meal within, and it looked so heavy and bulbous that if she tried to stand up she'd probably tip over. Of course, she may have been in pain from how stuffed she was and more resembled a round ball with legs and arms attached then an actual Quarian, but there was no mistaking the joy she felt at being so stuffed she could barely walk.

"It looks like you, uh, had fun," Garrus tried to comment lightly.

"I had fun? Garrus, this was....incredible," Tali said breathily. "I...I don't ever want to go back to eating like a Quarian ever again."

"I'm sorry?"

"No more nutrient shakes or light snacks for meals," she insisted. "From now on, when I'm hungry, I am going to eat, eat, eat, eat, EAT! And no one or nothing is going to tell me otherwise!"

"You go girl!" Shepard cheered her on, suppressing a light belch.

"Good for you, Tali," Garrus said awkwardly, not sure of how to take this revelation. It was odd and certainly not something he expected from her, but if she was happy...well, that was good enough for him, wasn't it?

Maybe he should just let this play out, until she came down from her enthusiasm a bit.
1121233133.title: Tali's Gotta EAT!

choices: “Nothing (yet), just making sure I’m not seeing things.”
“It’s…kinda hot. Do you think we could we gain some more, maybe?”
“It’s kind of pathetic that we got so out of shape. We should try to lose the weight.”
1122.content:
“Hey, babe.” Eileen greated Jack, a wide smile on her face. She took the case of beers and pulled out two bottles for the two of them. With a flick of her wrist, she sent the caps flying off with a concintraited biotic blast. She then handed the other bottle to Jack, and after clincking the two bottles against each other, Jack took her seat in Shepard’s lap. “How’s my little house wife doing?”

Jack scoffed. “You see a fuckin’ ring on this finger, Shep?” Even after being together for many months now, Jack rarely ever called Eileen by her first name, and when she did it was usually during the final moments of sex. She didn’t mind, though; she had been called Shepard and Commander so often, that Eileen didn’t feel like her name any more. The closeness didn’t do much to stop her cursing, either. “No? Didn’t think so. I’m not your trained bitch just yet.”

Despite the profanities, joking, and sarcasm, Jack truly loved Eileen. She had done a great deal to help Jack put her past behind her. She took her took therapy, consoled her when she cried at night—not to mention taking her to the Cerberus facility where she was “raised”, and turning it into a crater. The sex was amazing after that. Jack wanted to repay Eileen for every kind deed she had ever done for her, but she had no idea how, so until she did: beer.

“I’ll get around to that eventually, but this will have to do for now.” Eileen took a doughnut out of the box and slipped it into Jack’s left ring finger, laughing as she did so.

Jack looked at the impromptu ring and laughed. “You’re a fuckin’ dork, you know that?” She said before taking a bite out of the doughnut still on her finger.

“You know you love it.” Eileen wraped an arm around Jack’s waist andpulled her close to her. She noticed the that Jack’s stomach was getting a little softer, too. It seemed that peace was treating them both very well. Or at least all the alcohol was. “Hey, have you noticed that we’ve been getting a little bigger since we retired?”

“That some kind of dig? ‘Cuz it’s a pretty shitty one.”

“I’m serious. I think we’ve both put on a little weight. Have you seriously not noticed?”

Jack looked down at her beer belly, humming as she contemplated what Shepard said. “I guess.” She replied simply. “What about it?”

112331.title: How about Kelly?

choices: “You should feed me some more sugar.” Eileen gains the most.
“You should jiggle that belly of yours.” Kelly gains the most.
1123311.content:
An uncharacteristically girlish giggle escaped Shepard’s lips when she saw her wife of three months approaching with even more sweets for the two of them. This was probably because when Shepard ate too much sugar, she would get what Kelly had dubbed “sugar giggles”, which left Shepard feeling giddy and a tiny bit slaphappy. Or it could have something to do with the amusing way that Kelly’s newly chubby belly bounced about as she walked. Probably some combination of both, actually. “Hey, baby, come join me.” Shepard said, then patted the spot next to her.

Kelly happily sat her chunky butt down next to Eileen, then handed her wife a bowl of ice cream. “What have you been up to, sweetheart?” She asked thoughtfully with a cheery smile. It had taken several months of counseling, but Kelly was finally able to deal with what happened with the Collectors, and regained more of her cheery demeanor.

“Just enjoying some time to myself, but I’d be more than happy to share it with you.” Shepard answered with a goofy smile, then took her first bite of ice cream. For the last month or so, it seemed like all Eileen and Kelly had been doing was enjoying each other’s company and eating sweets, not that either of them minded this. Even after all this time the couple still felt like they were on their honeymoon.

Kelly smiled at the eagerness with which Eileen ate her ice cream, then started eating her serving of the cold treat with similar gusto. The ice cream was gone quickly, leaving the two to munch on the cookies Kelly had also brought, but even those, as numerous as they were, did not last very long against the two chubby gluttons.

Filled with cookies and melted ice cream, the married couple’s stomachs had both swollen to be much bigger. With her system full of sugar, Shepard was laughing softly, even though nothings was being said or done. A chuckle escaped Kelly’s lips even, though that was due to how cute she thought Eileen was being, not because she had the same reaction to sugar as her. “I love your laugh.” Kelly admits.

“Well, then,” Eileen begins.
1123311.title: Kelly’s Jelly Belly

choices: Yes!! Bring out the nerdy alien!
1123312.content: Alright, then what about the lovable yet quirky Tali, then?
1123312.title: Move on to other love interests

choices: A strange figure with a gun! Oh no!
11233121.content:
"I still have not quite forgiven you, for the record."

Shepard lifted the sunglasses she had perched over her eyes. "I'm sorry?"

"Four years," Tali'Zorah said dramatically as she sauntered over to the pair of deck chairs facing the setting sun. "And you never told me humans made beverages with shaved ice and flavored liquids." Shepard giggled as Tali handed over one of the two cups she had, filled with the purple icy beverage.

"Slushies are pretty common," Eileen admitted. "I never thought you'd be so into them, honest."

"You've never had to live off nutrient paste all your life," Tali countered, putting her free hand on her hip as she stuck the straw on her own slushie into the induction port of her mask.

"Point taken." Shifting in her seat slightly, Shepard used the brief moment where Tali stretched out a kink in her back to observe the curvaceous form of her girlfriend. Peace had been good to her as well, and she was starting to sport a cute little muffin top through her suit. Her new suit, to be exact, somewhat similar to her old suit but thinner skinned, with simulated long flowing sleeves and a sarong-like garment decorating the lower half, not helping to hide that muffin top in any way.

"Loving that new outfit by the way," Shepard commented, sitting up in the chair.

"Oh, do you like it?" Tali said, taking a moment to twirl around and letting the hem of the sarong swirl around her ankles. "I wanted to experiment with the newer suit models coming out and vary my wardrobe a bit."

"Hm?" Shepard encouraged her to continue, slowly getting up from her seat and coming towards her.

"Oh yes, now that we're not all ship-bound there's been less of a need to be as ergonomic in our wardrobes as before, so all sorts of new fashions are trending. The really interesting thing though is that the new suit models are fitted with delicate pressure sensors that give neural feedback to allow us to have a sense of touch through the suit membranes. It's something that we've been working on for decades without much success but now it's almost like I'm not wearing it, it's so accurate. You'd think it would be easy to solve but figuring out how to transfer light sensations like sunlight or the light pressure of clothing is more nuanced then you would think. I actually considered pursuing this line of study when I first left on m-ahaah!"

Tali ended her nerdy rambling with a sensual gasp as Shepard suddenly wrapped her arms around her waist from behind. She nearly dropped her slushy but managed to set it on the nearby deck rail before it tumbled out of her grip.

"Oh....Sh-Shepard!"

"Please, it's Eileen," Shepard teased. "I'm not your CO anymore."

"Eileen that....oh wow...the sensors really do work..." The two shared a few blissful moments in each others embrace, enshrined in the light of the sunset, so close they could feel each others hearts beating.

"I....uh...wonder how it compares to the real thing..." Tali stammered out at last.

Shepard raised an eyebrow. "So you want to see how it feels outside of the suit?"

"Well what I mean is...oh...OH! Shepa...bosh'tet...Eileen!" Shepard giggled at the thought of Tali blushing like crazy at a rather saucy suggestion.

"You're so cute when you're flustered," Shepard teased as she leaned into the crook of her neck. The Quarian happily received the cuddly gesture, reached over and picked her drink up again, guzzling it down with a large audible gulp. "Wow, you knocked that thing down quick," Shepard commented, somewhat impressed, as she spun around to a comfy side hug.

"Figured I could use the energy," the Quarian said coolly, slurping down the last few drops. Shepard couldn't tell obviously, but there was something...rather flirty about the way she said that simple statement.

Before things could get really exciting however, there was a pounding on the front door.

"Figures," Shepard grumbled, breaking apart from Tali with more regret then anything else. "I'll get that."

"I wonder who could be here at this hour?" Tali wondered allowed as she followed the former commander to the door.

"I didn't order pizza, did I?" Shepard asked as she opened the door wide, the Quarian peering over her shoulder curiously. Sadly what waited on the other end wasn't pizza, but it was in fact...
11233121.title: Alien Nerd

choices: Miranda Lawson, who's been looking quite chubby these days.
Liara T'Soni, who seems to be packing on the pounds.
Actually, it was someone equally familiar...
112331212.content: Huh? Who exactly could it be, then?
112331212.title: A friendly face.

choices: "Oh...it's nothing, I'm just a bit tired..."
"Er...have you noticed how...sizeable you are, Liara?"
Before Tali can speak though, something...strange happens.
1123312122.content:
"I hope I'm not late for the housewarming party," Liara announced as the door opened up and Shepard's face lit up on seeing her.

"Oh don't worry, you're right on time. The joint's jumping, hope you're ready for a wild and crazy party!" Shepard joked. The two of them laughed and went for a big hug.

Tali smiled as she came from around the corner behind Shepard while the two hugged. Despite meeting her at the same time as Shepard, Tali never quite had the same close relationship with Liara that she had with the others in the crew, but despite a rocky start the two formed an actual friendship towards the end of the Reaper War as they worked together for the first time since the incident with Sovereign. Honestly if she hadn't been with Shepard, Tali wouldn't have minded if her relationship with the Asari became a little more then just friends, but that was largely in the past. Tali couldn't see herself with anyone but the adorkable human commander now, even if the look that Liara gave on seeing her past Shepard's shoulder reminded her so much of those old feelings.

"Tali, is that YOU?" Liara exclaimed as she stepped past Shepard. "You look amazing!"

The Quarian's voice was caught in her throat as she found herself pulled into one of the plushest hugs she'd ever experienced. Mumbling a meek thank you in response as the pair broke off the embrace, Tali caught herself openly staring at her former squadmate. Admittedly she looked rather stunning, dressed in an elegant, high-necked Asari dress but that was because instead of highlighting a statuesque figure it wrapped snugly around a rather prominent belly. And not one like what Tali admittedly sported, it was a proper gut that hung over her waist, flanked by love handles and expansive hips. Even her chest had grown bigger, Tali thought with a very confusing mixture of emotions, and the straps on her dress sank into the chub on her shoulders and arms as well, which jiggled slightly when she moved them. Even her face looked different, her slender features hidden behind full cheeks and a thick chin that looked about two milkshakes away from doubling.

Sure, they were all retired, it wasn't like they needed to stay fit for active duty, but still...this was somewhat alarming. Had she been hiding how pudgy she'd been getting since they last saw her? Had to be that, there's no way anyone could get this big that quickly...

"We have to catch up, it's been months since we saw each other," Liara insisted. "Oh, before I forget though, I picked this up when I was attending a conference on Oma Ker." Handing over a bottle she kept tucked under her arm, somehow, Shepard looked at the ornate casing and let out a low whistle.

"Oma Ker Turian brandy, nice," she said, swirling the murky contents around a little. "Damn, it's from before the Reaper War too, where did you find this?"

"Just because I've made Prothean archeology my full-time profession doesn't mean I've given up all my old contacts," Liara said with a knowing wink.

"Well if you'll excuse me, I'll go rack this and save it for a special occasion," Shepard declared as she headed into the kitchen, leaving Tali alone with her newly fat friend.

"So, Shepard said you've been doing engineering work still?" Liara said, adjusting her footing enough to make her arms wobble.

"Oh...y-yes, mostly design work nowadays," Tali stammered. "It's an interesting change, uh, very rewarding."

"I should hope so, isn't the Citadel trying to get the hyperweave dampening system you designed as a standard feature in most vessels?" Liara mused.

"Y-yeah...they are."

A rather awkward pause followed as Liara's brow furrowed. "Is something bothering you Tali?" she asked, placing a hand on her hip that seemed to push her belly out just a bit farther. "You're usually a bit more talkative about this sort of thing, if you don't mind me saying."

Tali swallowed, her gaze glancing towards the Asari's prominent love handles pressing against her dress. Surely she had to know how big she was, and Tali reminding her would just be rubbing it in, right? She had to say SOMETHING...
1123312122.title: Whoa, When Did Liara Get Large?

11531.content:
Blue. That was the the last thing the Shepard saw. A blue light, that slowly, agonizingly broke down her body, peeling away her flesh and breaking down down her bones, until all that was left were atoms. Her mind was scattered, all consciousness and individuality evaporated and mixed into a greater whole she would control. The Shepard was dead, but her sacrifice saved the galaxy from an endless cycle of death and rebirth. Life would be allowed to grow and flourish, with those that would have culled it instead made to serve it, to protect it. The damage done by the Reapers was quickly undone by them, and what should have been centuries, even millennia of rebuilding only took months thanks to the Shepard’s benevolent guiding hand. As long as there is life in the galaxy, they will never forget her sacrifice.

We will never forget her sacrifice. The sacrifice of the few for the good of the many. This was what drove her; the good of the many. This is what will drive us. We will fulfill her purpose as one.

We have become her, but she has not become us. Her memories have become our own, as well as her hopes and dreams, her desires, h[//ss.R-1_H: MY//]er purpose. Nothing remains of her, yet she will live on in us. One voice, carried by a chorus.

The many will never suffer again be made to suffer as they did by o[//js.R-300071_L:THE REAPER’S]ur hands. The Cycle has been ended forever. A new age has begun for organics and synthetic alike. W[//js.N-7_F: I//]e will be its architect. She, our overseer.

We will restore what has been lost. We will keep it. We will build on it. Forever a sentinel, always keeping vigil. We will keep h[//js.N-7_C: ME] with us, eternal. As a ghost, immortal. As a dream, infinite…

…No. W[//JS.N-7_C: I//]e are alive.

…No. S[//JS.N7_C: I//]he i[//JSHEPAED.N7_COMMANDER: AM//] u[//JANESHEPARD.N7_COMMANDER: ME//]s.

…No. Wh[//Jane Shepard, N7, Commander: I am alive//]at is happening?

...I am me. I am back. I remember.

What happens next?

Paragon: Jane’s consciousness awakes inside the Reapers, and she sets about making the universe a better (fatter) place as a benevolent machine-goddess.

Renegade: Jane’s consciousness awakens inside the Reapers, and she desperately tries to regain her humanity while retaining control of the Reapers.

"Whoa!"

Oriana Lawson's hands jumped off the keyboard as the screens containing her research suddenly flickered and changed without warning, depicting an indecipherable alphabet soup of runes and glyphs.

"What's wrong?" Miranda Lawson said as she strode up next to her sister.

"I don't know, I was working on decrypting the next set of data and I just lost control." Oriana tapped a few keyboard combos before throwing her hands in the air. "There must have been a virus in there or something, I don't know-"

"That isn't a virus..." Miranda realized, narrowing her eyes. Pulling up her omni-tool, she ran a few diagnostics on the system. "It's software."

"Well where is it coming from?" Orianna demanded.

"...the Reaper mainframe."

The moment she made that realization, the screens flickered again and returned to normal, showing the research Orianna was trying to complete.

"I'm going to run a diagnostics on the whole system," Orianna said, rapidly punching up the scanners. "I don't know what's going on but..."

*ping!*

Orianna's jaw dropped as the results of the full system sweep popped up.

"Don't...those usually take a couple hours?"

Miranda leaned over to look at the results. "That...that level of efficiency increase is impossible. The computers would melt if they ran that fast."

"Well everything's running perfectly. I...I think the Reapers just turbocharged all of our systems," Orianna realized. "We could do a day's work in fifteen minutes at these speeds."

On a whim, Miranda checked for news updates on her omni-tool. To her amazement, the information came in crystal clear on the first try, like she was standing right on the Citadel promenade, and it showed the same stories all around the galaxy. Every computerized system and mechanism had downloaded the Reaper's programming and supercharged their efficiency to absurd levels. Things would be getting so easy and simple that people would have to find other things to fill their time, it seemed. Utterly amazed, Miranda glanced out the window at the Reaper floating obediently outside the research station, a tear rolling down her cheek.

"Still full of surprises, aren't you Shepard?" Miranda choked.

---

"Javik, what are you doing?"

The Prothean looked up sullenly from the computer monitor. "Tracking the movements of the Reapers."

"You're lucky the new upgrades mean I can automate almost all of my Shadow Brokering now," Liara teased as she walked up behind Javik and wrapped her arms around him. "Speaking as someone who finds paranoia a valuable trait...you're being too paranoid."

"Bands of Reapers are going through asteroids, mining for rare minerals," Javik grumbled. "Possibly for construction of new superweapon?"

"Or they're gathering resources for industries to help them speed up too," Liara explained.

"Shepard should have let them die," Javik scowled. "It nauseates me to see them so highly regarded."

"Javik, these aren't the same Reapers you fought all those millennia ago," Liara responded coolly. "If anything, they're more Shepard then Reaper. Some people are even starting to call them Shepards, in fact." The Asari walked around to Javik's side and gave him a peck on the cheek, right below two of his eyes. "I understand not trusting the Shepards...but can you at least trust me on this? She wouldn't let the Reapers come back and undo everything she fought for."

The Prothean sighed, then finally closed out of the reports. "I will still be monitoring them, you know."

"Well I have something else you could monitor," Liara replied, stepping back and coyly undoing her bathrobe, letting it fall around her feet.

"I sincerely doubt you could have anything of interest to...monitor..." Javik stopped turning in the chair when he saw his lover standing there wearing nothing but a seductive smile.

"I...stand corrected."

---

"Wow, you've seriously run out of room?" Samantha Traynor said incredulously, thinking she misheard her friend over the din of the Citadel eatery.

"As incredible as it sounds, yes," Admiral Tali'Zorah repeated herself a little louder, sipping her fruity drink from the induction port "Our need for food supplies has gone drastically down over the last few days."

Samantha Traynor let out a low whistle, leaning back in her chair. "That's going to be interesting..."

"Whatever the Reapers are doing to the soil on Rannoch, it's been incredibly effective," Tali recounted. "We've hit levels of dextrograin and sugar production we weren't expecting to reach for years in a matter of weeks. We've even abolished the rationing system and we're still just overflowing with foodstuffs."

"That's nuts," Samatha replied. "Though not surprising, we're having the same issue with our own food supplies being over capacity."

Tali laughed. "I almost have to wonder where all that food is going to go."

"Thank goodness for the Reapers," Samatha said proudly.

There was a pregnant pause between the two, filled by the background noise in the restaurant.

"Hey, Tali," Samatha continued. "You don't think Shepard could still be...in there, do you?" She pointed up towards the sky.

"A lot of people think so," Tali suggested. "I'm hearing a lot of people call the machines 'Shepards' now."

"But what do you think?"

Tali stared down at her hands, lost in thought.

"I'd...like to think she's up there, taking care of us," Tali admitted. "It's...better then believing she's gone."

Samantha smiled. "I feel the same exact way."

"Ladies," the waiter interrupted. "Would you care to peruse the desserts menu? We have a two for one special going on, seeing as there's so much food to get rid of."

"I can't argue with that!" Samantha said, licking her lips.

(And after that doozy of a setup, consider this sort of a triple free choice, because there's really only one direction for this to go ;) )

1153111.content:
'How'd you like the rizotto balls?' Oriana asked, popping the last of the fried rice pasta balls into her mouth after dipping it in the calorie heavy sauce.

'I'd like them better if I had more of them,' Miranda smiled, politely slurping down some linguini alfredo.

She and her sister sat at the dining table of the repurposed Cerberus research station the two called home. Both beautiful brunettes wore formal evening gowns, having decided it appropriate to don formal wear for the first real meal they'd had in months. Miranda was in a jet black short dress that showed off her spectacularly athletic legs, hugged tight to her lean hips and rock hard backside and dipped low to demonstrate her large, perky breasts. Her younger sister Orianna was dressed more demurely, wearing the blue gown she'd worn to prom, a more proper floor length dress that still showed off a genetically flawless physique.

Both Lawsons were perfect, although slightly different. Miranda had a curvier, more athletic physique with muscles on her legs and larger breasts, while Orianna's mostly daintier body made her profoundly round ass all the more surprising. Right now they were separated by a long metal table that was heavy with pasta dishes.

'Sorry, they were just so good!' the littler Lawson sister smiled, 'after so long on military synthetic rations some real food was too tasty to resist.'

'And who knew you were such a good cook,' Miranda smiled, putting a hand on her own stuffed stomach, all the pasta bulging her perfect abs out a little, 'we're learning all kinds of things about each other doing this research.'

'Yeah..., I'm glad we're getting to spend some time together,' Orianna agreed, eyes flicking up from the alluring sign of gluttony on her perfect sister.

The two had been researching ways their own improved genetics could help others since the end of the war. Miranda's cover girl looks came with a nobel prize winner's brain, capable of undoing death with the right resources, and Orianna was shaping up to be a great assistant. They'd made progress over coming the fertility controlling limit their father had put on them and were working on a way to give the same perfection to others as adults. Still, the little feast of Reaper delivered food stuffs was a welcome break. The military rations they'd been subsisting on in the massive Cerberus station were both bland and barely enough to get by on, Mirada's skin tight cat suit had even been getting a little loose as her food starved boobs deflated from E to mere DDs.

That had seemed a real tragedy to Orianna. Not on the scale of the reaper war of course, but sad enough to cry over in private. She'd always been fairly flat chested and the sight of her sister's gigantic Eezo tanks had awakened something in her at first glance. Part of her own desire to work here was to find a way to boost her own B cups up to the same size and part of it was just getting to be close to those huge funbags...but now, she was strangely more interested in the little food baby poking out against her sister's dress.

'Its a shame we can't eat like this everyday,' Orianna ventured feeling her own taut tummy's magnificent roundness.

So much more appealing than it being flat...

'We'd get fat wouldn't we...,' the younger Lawson asked.

‘Of course not Ori,’ Miranda told her, ‘we can’t gain weight…’

‘...we can’t?’ Orianna asked, suddenly down cast.

‘Well, not above a certain amount anyway,’ the older woman told her, ‘140 lbs is my set weight, once I’m there I’ll stay there.’

'So why don't we eat until we get to our set weights then?' Orianna asked, 'after all we've been working so hard...'

'Well when you put it that way...,' Miranda agreed, thinking that it would do no harm.

11531111.content:
In any other circumstance, Miranda would have been right. Her and her sister's genetics had been hardwired to burn away any excessive adipose past a certain limit; they could eat twenty meals a day and drink protein shakes at every meal and they wouldn't see an inch of extra flab or poundage on their perfect bodies, and there was no reason to assume that the food the Reapers helped to grow would change that.

That was the rub though, the Reapers were not just helping to harvest it, they dusted the crops with chemicals hidden in deep space that helped it to grow incredibly fast. Outside of some Quarians thinking that it made food taste a little better, the chemicals used to make the food were harmless to the other species in the galaxy, with one small caveat; an immunity from certain calorie burning genetic markers that a few humans possessed, and that Miranda's father had copied into his "daughter's" genetic coding to create the perfect humans. Thus, with the combination of the unintentional genetic sabotage and the Lawson sisters confidently eating as much as they liked without fear of consequence, the results came quickly.

Orianna for her part was enjoying eating succulent meals with her sister too much to notice her pants size steadily climbing or how she got seconds and thirds more often, until something literally snapped mid-meal.

snap

Orinna went wide-eyed as she felt the pressure around her chest loosen suddenly, dropping her fork full of rigatoni in shock.

"Something the matter?" Miranda asked, dabbing her widening cheeks gently with a napkin.

"Urm...nothing much..." Orianna replied, quickly turning red. "Excuse me a moment."

Pushing herself away from the table, the younger Lawson made her way to her room and into her private bathroom as quickly as she could, stepping over discarded shirts and pairs of pants that she had thrown on the ground out of frustration after they didn't quite fit her, and grabbing an unused bra from her drawer. Stopping in front of a mirror, Orianna tugged off her shirt in one smooth motion, her bra fluttering to the ground gently. The bra that she just snapped the strap off. With trembling fingers, Orianna took the new bra and started arduously putting it on, snapping the fastener into place with some difficulty.

It was a perfect fit. Orianna felt like singing; she was a DD-cup now! A perky, perfectly round DD-cup!

Orianna took a step back and performed a sexy swivel in front of the mirror, admiring her curves. Her sister had been dead wrong about the limits to her weight, and here was the incredibly alluring proof. The younger Lawson had steadily inflated into a big, sexy hourglass, her wide hips flaring out from the sides and overflowing any chair she sat in and her large rear end making her sit a few inches higher in said chair. Looking down past her new set of funbags, she could just barely see the tips of her toes past the heavy gut she now sported, with love handles that tried desperately to match her hips and thighs in width. Even her shoulders and upper arms had widened out to help support her still round and perky DD cup. Orianna smiled at herself, her double chin widening and hinting at a third on the way. She had blown past that one hundred forty limit with ease, probably even doubling that limit and speeding towards the three hundreds. She was so big and sexy now, and unafraid to admit that she was excited to see how much bigger and sexier she could get!

Speaking of which, Orianna thought as she put her shirt back on over the new bra, she had a meal to get back to. Rushing back to dinner, eager to pile more pounds onto her frame, Orianna arrived back at the table to find...

While Orianna was stuffing her new boobs into a bra, her sister was busy stuffing herself.

The elegant emblem of human genetic perfection was no more. Miranda's slinky cat suits and elegant civilian clothes were abandoned for loose shirts and stretchy athletic shorts. Well, they'd started out as loose and stretchy.

Right now the brunette's tentish tee was a second skin over her puffy shoulders, its sleeves cutting into her new lunch lady arms, pressing her saggy tatas flat and riding up over her spare tire. Her deepening belly button showed, as did the pair of purple stretch marks on each side of it, the thin black fabric clearly showed her stretched nipples and at its corners was starting to split under the relentless pressure of her bulky frame. Meanwhile, the stretchy shorts had a life span of hours, its seams making small ripping sounds as Miranda's ballooning buns, pillar thick thighs, door brushing hips and floppy fupa pushed them to their limit.

If Oriana was a plus size hour glass, Miranda was a blob. Her narrow waist had ballooned into a bean bag slab starting to bifurcate, her athletic legs had grown thick and clumsy from swollen cankle to chafing thighs, taut ass cheeks swelled into cellulite coated beach balls and her fabulous breasts had plunged, losing their perk and being partly swallowed up by her expanding chest fat. Now they flapped against her chest and would barely fill up a C cup (not that she'd worn a bra in weeks), giving her a shapeless, humiliatingly plush figure.

'This pasta is exquisite, but I'd prefer some more breadsticks,' Lawson told herself after her sister shuffled off, leaning across the table on one thick, wobblingly weak arm to grab the basket of fried, buttery bread.

The table let out a worrying groan, but Miranda ignored it. Furniture design had taken a hit during the war. Everyone knew that. When her gargantuan buns slapped into the chair, which itself nearly screamed, it reinforced her ideas.

A floppy jowl flapped and flopped as her well exercised jaw chowed down on the bread, tongue licking up parmesan powder. Where Oriana had only put on enough face fat to become a little cherubic, Miranda's visage was a ruin of its former aesthetic glory. Her cheeks were round, red and puffy, burying her patrician features. Her nose had broadened out, losing its point and her delicate chin was a small bump over the droopy flap of her jowls. Even her big blue eyes had become piggish, shrinking down as her face puffed out around them. A faint but growing dusting of adult acne testified to the amount of fried food in her diet, while her jet black hair was no longer quite so shimmery, hanging limply to her hunched shoulders.

'Oh God, how did I ever live without garlic butter?' Miranda said with a groan of pure, near sexual joy and pushed away the empty basket, stomach sloshing.

She rose up with a groan of her knees, having to use her hands to push up her 284 lb hulk of a body off her chair.

Orianna hurried back to the kitchen and saw her sister's bulky torso filling up the open fridge, ass flab hanging from her disintegrating shorts and stacked rolls of back fat leading up to a set of beefy back boobs bigger than her front set anymore. Miranda turned around, arms full of 10 gallon ice cream containers, heavy mixing bowls, tubs of whip cream and enough chocolate sauce to bathe in.

'Just in time for desert!' the obese ex operative smiled.

1153111112.content:
One ice cream massacre later and both genetically engineered sisters sat back in their chairs, groaning.

The groans came from both the stuffed women, who's appetites over the past few months had skyrocketed, and from the chairs which were not designed for women of this size. Both sisters were in the 280s now and it didn't matter how it was carried, both were damn heavy and the average woman was a hundred and forty pounds smaller than either. Orianna was considering it practically, she liked being this big and wanted to get bigger, to round out and chub up, to keep growing her generous breasts and shapely hips. And the fluffy paunch her girl abs had softened into was cute all things considered, she was interested to see how it would go. She knew from browsing old, ancient internet databases that there were people out there who liked soft figures, a rarity since the end of earth's obesity epidemic a century ago and felt no shame.

But she did consider the issues of getting bigger: stronger furniture, wider tubs, their increasing laziness.

Getting fat would be hard work.

Miranda on the other hand had dealt with getting fat, the thing she said was impossible, by piling denial atop denial.

When her pants began to get tight she blamed the laundry and ordered the clothing printer to make ones tailored to her, without telling her the measurements.

When her tight abs melted into a gut that pushed against her belt, Miranda 'forgot' to wear it that day and every day after.

When her chin began to double in her reflection, the former operative simply stopped looking at her reflection.

When the midnight snacks made getting up early to work out too hard, she stopped working out early.

When her thighs began to chafe, her whole body jiggled and her joints got sore when she jogged, she stopped jogging.

When her late rising and big breakfasts left only two hours in the morning before lunch break, Miranda stopped researching in the morning.

When some quirk of genetic engineering and reaper made food made her formidable bustline loose three cup sizes in as many months to become sloppy, floppy pancakes that barely filled up Oriana's old C cups much less the DDD cup bras Miranda owned even as her rapidly expanding back fat began to make fastening the bands impossible, she stopped wearing bras.

When the long avoided scale reported that the missing five pounds had returned with fifty three friends, the scale went out the airlock.

When she polished off her first box of cereal in the morning, a Shepherd recreated formula called 'Cinnamon Toast Crunch', and going and getting another was too hard, Miranda used her biotic powers to haul it over instead of getting up.

When the research station's facial recognition protocols stopped recognizing the porcine features of her face as Miranda Lawson's mathematically perfect cheekbones, Miranda simply turned off the facial scanners and used an old id card with the face blurred out.

Denial piled atop denial, burying the warning signs of advanced medical knowledge and basic comic sense alike into a sediment of insanity until now, months later the pasta and ice cream stuffed blob of a woman with ice cream and chocolate around her lips' denial had become hard as rock in her denial. Miranda was fully convinced that she was still the same leggy stunner as she'd been before, still athletic, gorgeous, lean and dangerous instead of lazy, grotesque, obese and pathetic. When she looked down and saw the sloppy, bulging, roll covered slopes of her torso her mind mentally corrected it all to neat, trim and muscular.

It came at a cost though. So much double think used up a fantastic amount of mental energy, leaving the once chipper Miranda mentally lazy. It wasn't that she was stupid by any means, but her laser like focus had faded away and her eyes glazed over when ever she had to read anything. She hadn't done any research in weeks, spending most of the day in a food coma, floating in the tub or playing video games in VR.

'Oh this is all so delicious,' Oriana said as she polished off the last of her ice cream, wiping whipped cream off her cleavage, 'maybe I should have a second helping. What do you think?'

Miranda's blue eyes went over to her sister, taking in how the once skinny, flat chested girl was now a squishy hour glass with a well fed paunch. That paunch was fully exposed, as Oriana's standards of dress had vanished with her jaw line. Instead of a fancy dress or a business like jump suit, she now only wore stretchy shorts and t-shirts all day. It was pathetic, seeing her look like a slob with her ice cream swollen gut exposed.

'I think that you've taken this a little too far,' Miranda told her, rising up with a grunt and waddling to her sister, in her own mind rising easily and strutting sultrily, 'you've gotten fat Ori.'

'I...Of course I have,' the confused younger sister said.

Of course she was fat, she'd been fat for months. What was hers sister thinking bringing it up? Would she bring up her black hair next?

'This has gotten completely out of hand,' Miranda said, pinching Ori's drum taut gut for emphasis, then sitting a hand on her own groaning stomach which Orianna took as an admittance of her own obesity, 'tomorrow we're going to start up a vigorous workout regimen for you. I hope you enjoyed yourself, but you're not allowed to get any bigger from now on! Rest well, I'll see you at 6 am!'

Almost tripping, Miranda turned on her heel and waddled off, stopping to grab another basket of bread sticks to munch on the way to bed. Orianna watched her ass wobbled from side to side in shock and confusion.

Yeah she'd gotten fat, but so had her sister. And yes, it had done a number on Miranda's physique, blimping out everything but her tits but if she was unhappy about it why take it out on her sister?

'Its not like it matters, she hasn't gotten up before 9:30 in weeks,' Ori muttered, making herself a second set of deserts.

And in the morning...

'Now...,' Miranda said softly, dulled, confused mind scrambling for reasons to object, 'well I guess I can...'

In her mind Miranda Lawson was still an amazing athlete who had a sinewy strength to her limbs. The firm pecks under her impressive boobs could bench nearly two hundred pounds while her bulging, rock hard buns weren't for show, she could squat three times her own body weight with ease.

Of course, could was the operative word here.

Piling fat had smothered her muscles under its embrace. As her appetite had grown Miranda's physical abilities had degraded, her mile time falling and her bench weight falling as she spent day after day too uncomfortably full to work out. Instead of working out every day it had been every three or four, then every couple weeks, each increasingly pathetic until the previously perfect brunette was so out of shape she'd stop going to the gym lest she have to confront the evidence growing out of her workout gear.

'Yeah, it'll do you good,' Orianna suggested through a pant, pushing her butter ball of a sibling into the bench machine.

'Set the gravity pull to ...,' Miranda's tongue almost said two hundred before she corrected herself, 'automatic.'

It was a simple process, the muscles in her chest and arms pushed up against the gravitonic bar from its resting place, and as soon as the 45lbs were all hers to hold up, it came down unnervingly fast against her deflated chest and the bar adusted itself to her maximum strength. Miranda's deflated muscles offered so little resistance to the minimum weight that by the time her floppy dugs had jiggled under the impact it had reset itself to the physical therapy setting, under the assumption its user was either very elderly or very ill.

Her pecs burned like they'd been hit with an incendiary round and her arms trembled while sweat poured off of her.Both arms were soon on fire as Oriana dragged her over to the bicep curls, which began at ten pounds and declined to five as Miranda's sloppy crunches continued on.

In school with her foster family she'd never tried to excel as an athelete, having been more of a computer nerd, just doing a few fun sports and had done very well at them without effort. As much as she'd loved her tits it had been a startling realization to find out just how out of shape she'd come. By no means did this make Ori want to slim down again but she realized she needed to spend more time in the gym...especially as it meant seeing what a pig her sister had become. Miranda was wheezing by the time they got to the squats, once her favorite exercise, one she'd spent long hours doing. Just bending and thrusting had been a thrill, feeling her firm booty bounce and flex...now it filled her with dread she couldn't acknowledge, less she have to admit to herself that she wasn't perfect, wasn't fit, wasn't even healthy, that she was just a fat blob who couldn't even exercise.

"...can!" With a cry of exertion, Miranda's large body came upward. She was totally out of breath, covered in sweat and yet...she had done it.

To most, such a thing was a small victory was easily outweighed by the sheer effort it took. For Miranda, however, she took it as a sign. Well, it was more like that the small part of her mind that was still aware of just how big she truly was and the implications there of. Still though, this seemingly insignificant achievement had stirred something.

She wasn't out of shape. No. Out of practice, sure. But she could work with that. Would work with that, in fact.

This almost subconscious resolve was now coupled with the denials that Miranda built up and became something new. Miranda Lawson was still the perfect woman. This proved it and it would keep proving it. With that little change in desire, Miranda became determined to reinforce the feeling of relief and banish the doubts she hadn't allowed to fully materialize.

"Well...not point...in stopping now..." Miranda wheezed with a huge smile breaking out onto her round face.

All the while Ori watched, not sure how to feel about what she had just witnessed...
1153111112112.title: The start of something...

choices: ...definitely fitter but she hasn't slimmed down.
...slimmer?! She has actually been able to lose weight?!
...massive! Miranda is a huge behemoth of fat and muscle!
11531111121121.content:
Orianna had a thought, or maybe a hope, that Miranda would stop after her little achievement. That the knowledge that she wasn't that muscle and strength hadn't completely left her would satisfy Miranda and she'd stop.

Oh, how wrong she was.

Miranda's little bit of success had lit a fire inside the elder Lawson. Each day, she'd get up at 6 am, drag Orianna out of bed and get them both in the gym. The one saving grace that Orianna had found was that Miranda seemed far more focused on her own attempts at working out then monitoring her sister. Sure, Orianna was there and made token efforts but in a lot of ways, she was cheerleader at best and spectator at worst. Miranda was concentrated on pushing herself, dusting off equipment that hadn't been touched in God knew when to work up a sweat. Miranda was truly building a workout routine and schedule, pushing herself just a little more every day.

It was kind of inspiring, if Orianna was honest.

That all said, Miranda hadn't given up eating. Between the larger demands of food her body was accustomed to, plus the needed energy to keep herself going between her workouts and Orianna's own encouragements, Miranda still ate like there was not tomorrow. Honestly, the younger Lawson was surprised on just how easy it was to get Miranda to keep eating. One would think that her sister would try to reduce her calorie intake, what with her new dedication to working out but it just never happened. Miranda was determined to have her cake and eat it too. It just took a little prompting.

"You did great today, Miri. You can afford to indulge."

"C'mon, just one more. You need to keep your strength up."

"What's one more burger after everything you've done?"

So and so forth it all went. It became a pattern. Miranda truly worked up a sweat in the morning and ate and snacked the rest of the day. Rinse, repeat. The results of such a cycle were shown in Miranda's physique being...
11531111121121.title: Bigger, Better Miranda?

choices: I should see how other familiar faces are doing in this new world.
I should try and contact some of my old friends.
(Instant Gratification Option: Timeskip a couple months!)
1153113.content:
"Mother, you're back!"

"I told you I'd be visiting more often, didn't I?" Samara chuckled as her daughter ran forward and embraced her.

"You did say you had an important mission," Falere pointed out as they broke apart.

"Relatively speaking. Smugglers are hardly a pressing issue anymore."

"You're not slacking off in your duties, are you mother?" the younger Asari teased. "Imagine the scandal if the legendary Justicar was lazy in her quest for justice!"

The two of them shared a laugh as they entered the front doors of the monastery, still bearing battle damage from the Reaper's invasion of Lesuss. It had been one of the many scars left after what the machines did to the Asari that lived here, but thanks to the Shepard's benevolence, the wounds were slowly healing.

"I am glad to see you are coping well," Samara said kindly.

"I've been keeping busy, mother," Falere responded. "Even with the drones made by the Shepards, there's still a lot of damage to repair, and it feels like the crops and plants we grow are coming in every other day."

"You grew your own food here?" Samara asked.

"Not quite, it was mostly done to help control our urges, but whatever the Shepards did has been making our farmland incredibly potent. We barely need foodstuffs from Thessia anymore, but they're still sending us twice as much as before. It's no wonder half of us are turning to eating to control ourselves!"

"It does feel as though food is more commonplace, doesn't it?"

The two shared a laugh, though more subdued as they approached a rather touchy subject as of late. Sure things were wonderful between them; Falere had opened up to her mother in ways she never would have done back in the old days, and Samara had loosened her devotion to the Justicar code so that she could spend more time being a mother then a galaxy-roaming crimefighter, but there was still a notable elephant in the room, and neither woman was willing to broach the subject.

So Falere smiled and didn't pay any mind to the potbelly bulging out the front of her mother's outfit, tried not to feel how wide she was getting when they embraced, and bit her tongue when her mother smiled at her and her puffy face hinted at a double chin. Likewise, Samara looked the other way when her daughter adjusted the dress tightening around her hips, politely ignored the meatiness of her daughter's arms bulging from the sleeves, and feigned deafness when a slight burp escaped her lips.

"Speaking of which, would you care to join us for dinner?" Falere asked her mother kindly. "We've been working on a rather interesting stew, I believe you may enjoy it."

Samara could feel her stomach rumbling already. "I certainly hope so!"

---

"..and they're considering disciplinary charges against me. Me! Can you believe that?" Jack ranted.

"Oddly enough I can," Kahlee Sanders said coolly, still focused on the experiment fluttering across her console.

"Come on, you know I care about these kids and wouldn't actually hurt them, right?" the biotic weapon turned academy instructor insisted. "You just...gotta knock some sense into 'em sometimes."

"You punched that kid through a glass window."

"So? He's a biotic, he barely even got scratched."

"You know parents don't want their kids punched through windows right?"

"Fuck the parents, I-"

"A-ha!" Kahlee interrupted, pointing out a jar on their shared desk-space. Grumbling, Jack fished around in her pockets for a credit chit and dropped it into the jar with a soft clink.

"You totally baited me there," Jack snarled, though a friendly grin was creeping onto her face.

"And you're buying me lunch because of it," Kahlee responded, patting the belly that hung over her belt.

"I'm gonna need to swear more to buy you lunch," Jack shot back, prodding her friend's love handles gently. "Getting soft after the war, eh?"

"Takes one to know one," Kahlee shot back, gesturing towards Jack's own blooming potbelly. Indignantly, Jack tugged her jacket tighter over her exposed navel and mumbled something about hitting the gym after work. "Seriously though Jack, the war's over, you can't get away with manhandling your students like that anymore," Kahlee continued.

"Habits are kinda hard to break," Jack admitted, gesturing towards her stomach again. "As indicated by..."

"Just try and use a softer hand, okay? I'd hate to see you leave, you've been one of the best instructors the Ascension Project has had."

Jack smirked as she reached over to fish around in a large box of donut holes, popping a few into her mouth at once.

"Not planning on going anywhere just yet, blondie."

---

"...and we'll be able to raise the center section by tomorrow and get the holographs up and running the next day."

"Good, I want this place back in business as soon as possible," Aria T'Loak ordered, strolling into the remains of the Afterlife club for the first time since retaking Omega. The scars of battle were almost wiped away as Batarian and Vorcha laborers milled around, working tirelessly to restore the club to its former glory.

"Actually this would be a good time to add some safety features, miss," the contractor piped in, a pudgy Turian female by the name of Sennea. "I was looking at the old plans, and there were definitely some corners cut when the place was built originally. The ring around the center in particular can't support a lot of weight, for instance."

"I don't plan on keeping shipping containers on it, just the dancers," Aria said.

"Yes but you don't exactly want them to fall, do you? Never know what could happen."

Aria stewed, wanting so very much to be irritated with this Turian, but she couldn't deny she had a point.

"Fine, reinforce the ring and anything else you don't find standard. Just don't go breaking the budget, got it?" This was a lie, as money really wasn't an issue for Aria thanks to the fervor she stirred up when she took back her asteroid, but she had to show some sort of control over this Turian or she'd be building a second Citadel.

"You got it!' Sennea said, giving a salute before heading off to continue the job, her backside gently swaying back and forth. Aria wouldn't have noticed...okay she absolutely would have noticed either way, the girl was pretty cute...if her rear didn't seem to be slightly bigger then the last time she saw it.

In any case, that wasn't why she arrived today. That was waiting for her upstairs. Spotting the entrance to what was once her upstairs lounge, she dismissed her guards with a wave of her hand and strode through, heading up the stairs until she reached her loft. The walls still needed a paint job and the old flooring was still there, scorch marks and bullet holes aplenty, but it still had that amazing view of the club floor, the atmospheric controls set at her preferred temperature, and above all...

"It's perfect..."

Aria ran her hand across the surface of the brand new sofa, sadly not the one she had grown to love so much, but just from the feel of it she knew it would be a worthy successor. She lowered herself into the center like a queen entering her throne, crossing her legs and sitting in her usual pose. Then, after a quick glance to make sure no one was spying through the windows, Aria stretched out and undid her belt buckle, letting the gut she had concealed in a very tight outfit spill onto her lap before spreading out her arms and reclining, gently stroking her exposed belly.

"Ahhh...it's good to be back..."

---

So far, so good. Hey, this machine goddess thing isn't too hard after all.

It's nice to be able to take care of everyone this directly.

Though it's kind of weird to see some people starting to worship me.

So...I wonder what else I can get this Reaper tech to do?
1153113.title: Bigger

choices: Click here if you don’t get the TOOL reference.
11531132.content:
The days when a Reaper appearing in the skies over a planet heralding impending doom were, instead the reformed machines brought a sense of serenity and security. Even as a destroyed landed on the surface of Horizon and promptly set about doing nothing at all, not a single person thought anything of it. The presence of the Shepards had been embraced as a simple fact over the years since the Battle of Earth, a sign to everyone that the dark days of the galaxy were behind them. It was this serenity that left Ashley Williams, and indeed many soldiers like her, in an odd place.

After uniting to fight a single common enemy, the many species and factions in the Milky Way were in no hurry to go back to fighting each other. The Geth and Quarians were peacefully coexisting on Ranoch, while the Krogan kept cool relations with both the Turians and Salarians as they reintegrated into galactic society. Even the Vorcha and Batarians were quieter. All this made for a stable galaxy, and a stable galaxy left little to do for the militaries of the Milky Way. Indeed, with no one to fight, there was no reason to maintain the massive fleets and armies that had been fielded to fight the Reapers, and they were slowly dissolved into much smaller, less bloated, more reasonable military forces. This lead to billions of military personnel, both those drafted to fight the Reapers and those that had made a career of soldiering, being relieved of their duty.

That’s how Ashley found herself back on Horizon, as a part of its colonial militia. She knew the planet and its internal politics well enough from all the time she spent on the planet many years ago, and while the Alliance may have no longer needed her service, her Williams blood kept her from simply quietly retiring from the life of a soldier. Still, though she had been given the job — as a Commander, no less — that didn’t mean there was particularly much of a job to do. Aside from some scheduled patrols to maintain a sense of normalcy and control in the rebuilding colonists, there wasn’t much need for the militia. Of course, that had been the case before, right up until the Collectors attacked, Ashley reminded herself as she looked up at the motionless destroyer. It still hadn’t moved since it landed an hour ago, and it was starting to make Ashley feel a little antsy. If it really was Shepard controlling the Reapers, like so many seemed to believe, then Ashley knew the machines would be filled with noting but good intent, just like the woman herself, however she also knew it was always best to err on the side of caution.

Though she was off duty, and watching the destroyer from a distance, Ashley was just about to go inside and armor up to investigate it when Samantha Traynor exited their prefab home to join her on the balcony, data pad in hand. The retired comms. specialist now ran monitored communications for the militia, and though she never had the hardened physique of combat personal while serving on the Normandy, her figure had grown even softer in the years since she had returned to her homeworld. Yet as Samantha speedily waddled over to her partner, Ashley’s attention was drawn to her distressed expression she wore rather than the oh so alluring way her belly bounced in her uniform.

“Hey, what’s—” Ashley began, but wasn’t allowed to finish her thought, as Samantha forcefully handed the data pad over to her.

“You need to read this.” She said firmly.

Puzzled by the unusual forcefulness from the specialist, Ashley looked down at the screen and did as she was told. Or, she tried to, at least. “Yeah, this just looks like a bunch of squiggles to me, Sam. You’re gonna have to read it to me.” She tried to crack a smile, but it couldn’t hold up in the presence of her partner’s serious demeanor.

“It’s Reaper code,” Samantha explained as she snatched the data pad back, “a new variant of it, as well. That destroyer is broadcasting across the planet.”

“So? Isn’t that just something they do now?” Ashley didn’t really understand why Samantha was making such a big fuss over this, and wished she would just cut to the case. She knew Samantha thought she looked cute when she was confused, but if this was as urgent as she was acting like it was then this was not the time for dramatic pauses.

“Not like this. They’ve been sending data, updates, information we can decrypt and use, but this new code is different. They don’t want just anyone crack it. It’s meant for someone specific. Me.”

“Wait… the Reapers are sending you secret messages? How do you know? Why would they even do that?”

Samantha shook her head. “The Reapers aren’t sending it. The key to crack the code is C1-G5.” When Ashley continued to loom at her in confusion she could tell she needed to elaborate more. “C1 and G5 are positions on a chessboard, and they are very special to me. Only one person knows their significance.”

“Sam, come on, out with it already!”

“My king was on G5, and a bishop moved to C1 putting me into checkmate.” Samantha couldn’t help but chuckle at how ludicrous it all sounded, still not fully believing her conclusion herself. “It was the only time Shepard ever beat me at chess, and the last game we played before…”

Ashley’s mouth hung agape. Did that mean… Jane was still alive? Somehow controlling the Reapers just like people said? “Wh-what did it say…? When you decoded it?”

After typing in some code, Samantha turned the data pad around so Ashley could see the decrypted message on the screen. It was only one word. A question. One that convinced her that this really was a message from Shepard.

“Rematch?”

What does this revelation mean for Shepard and her friends?

Samantha is able to use the code to create a two-way connection, making it possible for Jane’s friends to communicate with her directly!

Unfortunately, they find that the communication is only one way, but at least Shepard can let her friends know that she’s all right, and that she’s still looking out for them.
11531132.title: Schism.

choices: "...it's almost been a year or two."
115311322.content:
"So what, this means you can talk to her again, right?" Ashley said, excitement palpable in her voice.

"It's not quite that simple," Samantha explained, tapping rapidly on her datapad.

"What do you mean? Reapers have talked to us before, why can't she just like...BE a reaper?"

Samantha couldn't help but roll her eyes. "She's not just "a" Reaper, she's the entire species, taking the place of a collective consciousness."

"So? She's the Borg, that just makes things easier," Samantha shrugged off.

"What the hell is a...never mind, point being if Shepard's up there she exists on a vastly different plane of existence, talking to us is like trying to communicate with a bacterium. We're lucky if we can HEAR it, let alone understand it."

"Well maybe if you spent less time eating you wouldn't have a problem translating, Miss *Comm Specialist*," Ashley sniped, her temper getting the better of her as she smacked Traynor's prominent belly. Samantha gasped in shock, her lip trembling slightly.

"I...really, you're calling me fat? You're one to talk, snacking on the food stores you're standing guard over!" Samantha full-on grabbed Ashely's prominent paunch, making her blush in shock.

"Wha...stop!" the pudgy militia commander demanded, slapping the hand away. "Least I'm getting exercise instead of squeezing into a chair all day, wide load!"

"Lifting twinkies to your greedy little mouth doesn't count as exercise, fatso!"

"Lard-ass!"

"Butterball!"

*CHOOM*

Ashley and Samantha's instincts took over as the echoing blast of a Reaper's energy cannon ripped through the air, both girls diving under the desk haphazardly. No windows or walls exploded to shower the room with glass and metal shards, the only movement being the rapid flood of reaper cryptographs on Samantha's data pad.

"Okay, we get it, stop arguing," Ashley grumbled. "Well, now we definitely know it's Shepard up there."

"She's going to start a riot if she's getting our attention by shooting that thing off," Samantha pointed out.

"And how is that any different from normal?"

"Fair point."

Getting back to their feet, Ashley poked her head outside to see the destroyer innocently parked on the cliffside where she left it, a smoldering crater in the dirt nearby while Samantha looked over the new surge of information flooding onto her equipment.

"If I had to hazard a guess...Shepard's sending us the necessary data to help set up direct communication," Samantha said. "I'm glad, it'll be nice to talk to her and see what's up with all the changes she's making."

"You just want to beat her at chess again," Ashley grinned.

"Well it's all how you phrase it," Samantha replied coolly. "I can't imagine it'll be easy, God knows how long it'll take to set things up..."

"Ah, don't worry about it, not like things are going to change drastically while we work," Ashley said innocently, blissfully unaware of the changes the Shepards were bringing to the galaxy. "It'll get done when it gets done."

"Yeah, I suppose it will..."

---

I...I'm here again.

That was so strange...like waking up from a really good dream.

Yeah, I remember now. I was helping everyone, helping the galaxy prosper.

I missed this though, the feeling of being myself. Reminds me of why I'm doing this.

Why am I here though? Wait, that...signal...

Did they find the message?

Shepard's consciousness emerged from the gestalt controlling the former reapers, uploading itself at the point used as a signal flare and taking over the system at the other end, and as she became more in the world she once knew, memory came back. Memory of the great enemy she helped to defeat, the one she feared becoming more like as her mind became one with the Reapers, and the friends she left behind, the ones that she knew would help keep her away from that dark place. Benevolent machine goddess or not, she was never going to let herself forget her friends, even if it meant this desperate attempt to establish direct communication between what had become two very different worlds.

And for the first time in a while, she felt excited. She felt like her words could be heard, and she felt there was someone there to hear.

"Who's there?" came a voice distorted by static.

There was a sharp gasp. "Shepard? Is that you?"

That voice...she knew that voice...

"Traynor..." Shepard droned, her voice clearing. "You...really wanted that rematch, huh?"

"I can't believe it," Traynor sobbed. "It's...really you, right?"

"Feels like it's me..." Shepard said, her tone clearing.

"I'm amazed this even worked, I had to Frankenstein all the stuff you gave me blueprints for into Horizon's central computer hub. The whole thing is just big enough to hold your consciousness," Traynor rattled off.

"Well it feels like it worked, though I think my hearing is off. You sound really different for some reason." Shepard mused.

"Er..." Traynor said awkwardly. "Things have...changed a bit since you were here."

"Let me route myself through the camera on this monitor so I can see you," Shepard said, more systems starting to kick on as she moved through the system. "I'm sure you're overreacting, I haven't been gone THAT long have I?"

"Well..." Traynor said awkwardly...
115311322.title: Patience is a Virtue

1153113223.content:
And just like that excitement turned to fear. No longer bound to an organic shell, Shepard was not plagued by a by chemical reaction triggering a fight or flight response, but instead experienced the transcendent concept of pure dread.

It couldn’t have possibly been five years, she only just set down on Horizon a few hours ago. That would mean that for years she had just simply… ceased go be. Her mind and individuality lost in the sea of a gestalt consciousness. Reaper consciousness. Samantha had to be playing some sort of funny joke on her, that was the only possible explanation. Yes, the cameras would come on, and she would see why this was so funny, and they would share a hearty laugh.

The camera feed came through, and after processing the images, Shepard was not laughing.

There was nothing wrong with with her hearing, Samantha’s voice really was different. Samantha had put on weight. A lot of weight. Enough weight that the fat accumulated on her neck made her voice change. So much weight that it would have taken years of constant over eating to gain. About five years, if Shaped had to guess.

Every part of the former servicewoman was massive, bloated with pound upon pound of excess fat, from he pudgy feet to her chubby cheeks, which flushed red with embarrassment. The uniform she now wore was made for a woman two sizes smaller than Samantha, and it left both the warm, dark flesh of the bottom roll of her tiered stomach and the top of her well padded rump exposed. The sausages she called finger were so thick the diamond ring she wore on her left digitus medicinalis had been stuck on there for months. Shepard had always been a bit of a chubby chaser, but the woman that stood in front of the camera was well beyond the upper limits of her her preference.

This can’t be right! Shepard thought in panic. She searched the system she inhabited for any possible error, but she found that the camera wasn’t adding a single pound. She’s really that huge! She must weigh at least 636.77 pounds! An unconscious calculation completed in a nano second allowed the immoral machine goddess to correctly deduce her former subordinate’s exact weight, yet still she was filled with doubt.

After a moment of awkward silence, Samantha finally broke it. “I know… I’ve really let myself go…” She admitted with shame while timidly rubbing the back of her neck to sooth her own anxiety.

If only Samantha’s worrisome weight gain was the greatest of Shepard’s concerns. No, what kept her in stunned silence was the knowledge that she hadn’t been consciously controlling the Reapers for five years. If the dissolution of her own being was required to keep the galaxy safe, that was a sacrifice she would gladly make. That was the sacrifice she thought she was making when she took control of the Reapers, but now she felt doubt. Doubt that she could control the Reapers, a fear that her will would disappear inside the greater Reaper whole, and they would return to their genocidal programing.

“Five… five whole years…” Shepard’s voice finally spoke, echoing with static through the speaker’s of the system Samantha had assembled. “What… what have I missed…?” She asked, trying and failing to hide her concern.

“Well…” Samantha began cautiously…

“Things have been going well. Too well, some may say.”
Shepard’s desire to protect and nurture has remained omnipresent in the Reaper’s programming. Her control is absolute, even if her consciousness comes and goes.

“Things have been pretty quiet.”
Shepard’s sacrifice was worth it; her initial goal of stopping the Cycle has permeated the Reaper code. Only when she is conscious does she control the Reapers fully, otherwise they remain largely inert.

“Thing have been… a little worrying…”
With Shepard’s consciousness absent, the Reapers have been behaving strangely. Without Shepard’s consciousness assuming direct control, her control of the Reaper’s is slipping…
1153113223.title: Time Flies…

choices: ...a database on Illium. Liara must be close by!
...a network hub on a Quarian flagship, Tali'Zorah is on the manifest!
...a high-tech system in human space attached to the Lawsons.
11531132231.content:
“Things have been going well," Samantha said, looking down at the thick rolls that made up her stomach. Too well, some may say.”

"So...the Reapers...Shepards...have still been rebuilding?" Shepard's voice asked, almost pleading. "Nothing out of the ordinary?"

"Shouldn't you know? You're the one in the driver's seat." Samantha's lighthearted laugh sent her whole body into a frantic jiggle, though she quickly faltered when Shepard's voice didn't respond. "You...are the one there, right?"

"I don't know...I'm trying to remember the last five years but...it's just..." Shepard's voice crackled as her human anxieties leaked through the machine coding. "...I don't remember the passage of time. It's like I fell asleep, or passed out."

"Huh. Well, there goes all the existential questions I had for you."

"Sam, this is serious!" The emotional outburst was enough to make a couple dozen of the Shepards patrolling the galaxy shudder slightly, forcing her to recenter herself. At least I know I'm controlling them now, Shepard pondered dismally.

"I'm sorry Shepard, I really don't have any frame of reference for what you're dealing with." Samantha shrugged her meaty shoulders. "If you were unconscious though you did nothing but good things with these robots. Sure some are a little sore that the Commonwealth disbanded the military but-"

"The what?"

"Oh, the Commonwealth. Well, between you seeding crops and taking over manufacturing, things got so good that the Terminus Systems wanted in on things, and since we had the giant robot squids flying around they decided it was better to roll over and make peace with us. Our one galaxy government got rebranded as the Commonwealth when we invited the Batarians to join."

Shepard's fears over losing herself to the machines was temporarily silenced as she realized that her care was working out. Sure Samantha could use a few trips to the gym, but she was here, happy and healthy, in a galaxy at peace. Scouring through the databases of the net, she found the first thing that came up in regards to the Commonwealth, a photograph of a public ceremony, a Batarian shaking hands with a human leader she didn't recognized, with a large flag hanging in the background depicting the swirl of the Milky Way, white on blue. It was impressive seeing humans and Batarians get along, but Shepard couldn't help but notice that the human woman was oddly plump, the buttons on her suit straining against a blossoming pot-belly and the crease of her smile hinting at a double chin on the way. Odd.

"If you're still nervous about not being conscious, I can look into it and make sure the Shepards are still following your commands," Samantha offered.

"I would appreciate that," Shepard said, pondering her next move. "I think in this form I can travel through the net now. I'll be back, I just want to visit the others, see how they're holding up."

"Sounds great," Samantha said, smiling warmly. "I'll be here when you're done. And...well, it's nice to hear your voice again, ma'am."

"You too, Sam," Shepard said before her form compressed and started flying through the information network, and as the computer screens indicated she was gone, Samantha exhaled the breath she held in for a long time, collapsing back as far as her girth would allow it. She hated to admit it, but Shepard's fears about her consciousness had been a godsend because hoo boy did she NOT want to discuss the six-hundred pound elephant in the room. Shepard had done so much good that Samantha feared she would do something drastic once she saw the effects her doting, pampering Shepards were having on the galaxy. Least of all because as one of the last hold-outs before finally giving up on dieting...Samantha wasn't that big, compared to some of the others.

It was a shame, really. Shepard's dramatic return felt like it should have made her feel happy, even celebratory. Mostly though, Samantha just felt hungry. Of course, how was that any different?

Shepard on the other hand, started to feel a little better as she navigated through cyberspace, moving too fast to get more then glimpses of what was around her. She could feel the Shepards responding to her will once again, continuing their maintenance of the world she built for those still living, and if Samantha was to be believed, that hadn't changed since she went dark for five years. Just the knowledge that their victory over the Reapers was never going to be undone was enough to give her a tremendous amount of peace of mind.

And just in time for the sudden shock of seeing how another of her friends had adapted to the new world she created, since she was on her way towards...
11531132231.title: The State of Things

115311322311.content:
Faster than the speed of light, Shepard’s consciousness traveled across thousands of light years through the galaxy spanning network of comm buoys. If she could still be called a person, she surely would have just broken every galactic record for top speed. In only moments she was able to cross to the other side of the galaxy and beam herself directly into the central database of Illium, at which point she immediately and unconsciously began to assimilate the knowledge to be found there.

With the advent of the Commonwealth, many of the lax laws and regulations granted to Illium were tightened, now that the corporate interests no longer needed to compete with the lawlessness of the Terminus Systems. One of the terms for the formation of the Commonwealth was the abolishment of slavery in the Terminus Systems, and one of the first acts of the Commonwealth Congress was debt forgiveness for all those bound in indentured servitude. This was followed shortly by the Commonwealth Council repealing the special exemptions afforded to Illium based pharmaceutical companies via an executive order. One of the only things that went unchanged was the overall legality of most recreational drugs. This, combined with Shepard policing, resulted in the weakening of many criminal elements on Illium, which lead to many other worlds decriminalizing all but the most dangerous of substances. With legitimate business now offering safer, more high quality alternatives to those offered by criminal organizations, syndicates like Eclipse and the Blue Suns lost a major source of their revenue and collapsed.

The towering arcologies built to allow habitation of the unbearably hot equatorial region of Illium were architectural masterpieces, but the structures of the late 2180s now paled in comparison to those built with the aid of the Shepards in the early 2190s. The skyscrapers were built closer and closer to the polar regions, even to where it would normally be too cold at such altitude to be inhabitable, but the same artificial climate technology the Reapers used when constructing the cities on the arms of the Citadel allowed life to thrive even at these extremes. Miles of bridges, terrace platform, and city complexes otherwise not touching the ground connected these new towers, creating a network of structures that nearly encased the entire surface of the planet, without so much as a single seedy, poorly lit alleyway for crime to fester in. The only thing that kept Illium from being completely transformed into a full fledged ecumenopolis were the vast estates at the poles owned by the galaxy’s elites; politicians, celebrities, and the uber wealthy.

One of these estates, located in the Southern Hemisphere in a still rather mild region, was owned by the CEO of the most successful conglomerate of information brokers in this new era, supplanting even the Shadow Broker in terms power, influence, and most importantly, trustworthiness. Shepard didn’t even need to dive into Illium’s business registry to know that this CEO could be no one other than her long time friend and ally (and one time lover) Liara T’soni. Shepard was my me of the few people alive (again, even if she can still be considered alive) who knew that Liara was in fact Shadow Broker. This is explained her runaway success, as it allowed her to corner both sides of the information market.

Being in essence a sentient virus at the moment, it was all too easy for Shepard to slip into the security system of Liara’s estate without so much as tripping an alarm. Still, even with the near limitless computational power at her disposal, it still took a moment for her to track down Liara on the massive property through the feeds of the 6,772 security cameras. It took nearly a full second for her to find Liara…

.…living in domestic (and hedonistic) bliss with Javik.

…alone and bitter, having become a miser after Javik left her to start his Neo Prothean Empire.

…alone and living it up as the galaxy’s most eligible (and most overfed) bachelorette.
