In the solitary second it took Shepard to track Liara down, Shepard also had her mind thoroughly blown. She never really saw where Liara lived back in the day, but based on her quarters in the Normandy and her old workplace on this planet, she expected something sparse, businesslike, classical.

She certainly wasn't expecting anything like this. Rooms of marble and platinum, wide open skylights letting in the evening sun to glisten off the gleaming surfaces. Mechanical servants darted between rooms and up and down gilded staircases, past ornately made furniture in every style Shepard's mechanical database could look up in the milliseconds she had to enjoy it. She saw great libraries five rooms wide, a half-dozen indoor pools, an armory loaded with weapons both ancient and modern, long hallways with famous paintings mounted on the walls, a garage the size of a ship filled to the brim with exotic hovercars and jets, and two animal enclosures with animals both rare and common from across the galaxy. Two! In fact, the only thing that Liara's elaborate Disneyland-esque mansion seemed to really lack was Liara herself.

No, wait, there she was! A security scan marked her as still being in a rec room on the bottom floor! Shepard quickly jumped through the camera network until she found the room. Again it was quite impressive, with a giant plasma monitor nearly taking up one wall of the room and tower full of electronic equipment and games of all sort. Shepard couldn't help but go full nerd as she looked at her setup; had Liara picked up all the old retro games Shepard loved to wax nostalgic over? Gods, if she wasn't visiting she'd pull a Geth from somewhere and assume direct control just so she could play Super Mario World. And it wasn't just old school either, she had plenty of newer tech, even a hologram projector for the room!

Shepard did notice, however, that there was something different about this room compared to the others; it was used. Sure it was clean and comfortably lit thanks to automated systems, but the game systems and electronics were stacked haphazardly, and that couch in the center with snacks piled around it looked plenty worn as well, even bowed a little in the center. Sure Shepard loved her classic video games, but out of everything in this crazy mansion this was what Liara seemed to spend a lot of time on?

*Sluuurp*

Speak of the devil, that must be her. Shepard turned the cameras slightly to get a glimpse of-

"Ulp! Ugh...why are these doors....so small?"

You know, after seeing what happened to Samantha, this really shouldn't have shocked her as much as it did. But as Liara T'Soni huffed and puffed before finally extracting herself from the doorway, Shepard felt like she was having a stroke watching what was her old squadmate enter the room.

Liara...Liara got really fucking fat.

Shepard watched as Liara lifted a plastic drinking cup the size of her forearm, guzzling as delicately from a straw as one could muster with those fat chipmunk cheeks and an extra row of chin beneath her flabby jowls. She let out a satisfied sigh and used her chubby fingers to scratch the lowermost roll of her belly hanging out of a too-small hoodie flecked with old stains and smears of color from all the food Liara must be scarfing down. It only came up to her upper arms anyway, revealing how her flabby arms were starting to overtake her elbows, and the zipper was straining to hold on against her melon-sized breasts...god, what size were they, O-cups?

Letting out a small little belch, Liara started waddling over to the couch again, her thighs brushing together with each step. At first Shepard wondered if she was naked from the waist down, not even able to fit socks over those pudgy calves, but she was just wearing an absolutely massive pair of panties hidden from the front by her love handles. Shepard watched her tug the hoodie down slightly lower, utterly failing to hide not only the giant heart on her butt but the lowermost roll of her back fat, before she plopped down on the center of the sofa with a tremendous thud.

"Ahhhh, much better," Liara sighed, rooting around in the snack pile that took up the small amount of space in the three-seater(!) that she didn't fill on her own. Smiling, she pulled out a large can of whipped cream, popped the top off and sprayed her mouth full of the stuff as she picked up a controller with her other hand. "Wath your ath, Bowther!" Liara declared, flecking white across her large chest. "Ahm comin for you!"

This...this was too much. Shepard had to step in before she had the vast machine intellect equivalent of an aneurysm. Whipping up a holographic projection in mere seconds, resembling her old self but neon and hollow looking like the VI's on the Citadel. With an electronic pop, Holo-Shepard appeared at Liara's side, a strained expression on her face. Liara glanced lazily up at her.

"Oh...hey Shepard," she said, taking another swig of her drink. There was a painfully long pause before Liara's eyes suddenly shot open and she did a spit-take. "SHEPARD!" Liara gasped. "You...you came back!" For a brief moment, Shepard almost forgot the shocking transformation her friend went through at the sheer joy on Liara's face, before it turned purple after realizing she wasn't wearing pants.

"I did...figured out a way to poke my nose back into this level of reality," Holo-Shepard explained, somewhat stilted. "Or, something to that effect."

"I will take your word for it," Liara said. "Dimensional shifting was never one of my strong suits." The two of them laughed, though Holo-Shepard had trouble keeping her eyes off of how much the Asari wobbled. Maybe she'd broach the subject delicately.

"So...how are you and Javik holding up?"

Something twitched in Liara's expression. "We...we're not."

"...oh," Holo-Shepard said quietly. "What...what happened?"

"It's complicated." Liara sighed as she sunk into the sofa a bit lower. "Things were okay, but Javik was away so often. He wasn't comfortable with the Reapers flying around so he rarely came home, and he was so busy working on the Neo-Prothean Empire." She sniffled, wiping at her eyes a bit. "Few years ago we decided to see other people."

"I'm so sorry..."

Liara shrugged, trying her best to smile. "It's fine, I understand why he wanted to leave. Still hurts a bit, but...well, I'm happy enough here anyway. Nothing but me and my video games. And my multi-trillion dollar corporate empire, I suppose?"

"No wonder you could afford the comfort eating," Holo-Shepard laughed.

Liara blinked, looking confused. "What do you mean?"

Well, in for a penny. Holo-Shepard gestured towards Liara's flabby self hanging off the edge of the couch. "You uh, put on a few, you know? Really gave Samantha a run for her money."

Liara's brow furrowed. "You...you haven't been aware of what's been going on the last few years have you?"

"How did you...?" Holo-Shepard trailed off.

"Shepard...it's not just me and Samantha, you know that right?" Liara continued. "Everyone got fatter since you joined with the Shepards.

Holo-Shepard's jaw dropped. "The...the whole team got fat?"

Liara shook her head, her meaty jowls wobbling. "No Shepard...everyone."

Paragon: Shepard keeps her cool and, despite serious misgivings, listens to Liara explain what happened.

Renegade: Shepard freaks out. That can't be true, and she's going to go and see for herself how the galaxy has fared since then!

Other: In case this needs to get even weirder.

Everyone !” She enunciated every syllable to really get it across. “Every single person in the galaxy is morbidly obese, at least. Most people are so fat they needed a new term for it: ‘hyper obese’. Even the animals are fat now. Even the bugs…” At the thought of the galaxy’s growing insect population, Liara shuddered with revulsion, causing ripples of fat to cascade throughout her entire body, from her meaty jowls down to her hanging cankle fat.

Shepard just stood there in silence, dumbstruck by the revelation. Well, not necessarily stood, but rather manifested her image there in silence.

Regardless, it accomplished what Liara intended and snapped the digital presence of her former Commander out of her internal dialogue. Thankfully, Shepard was polite enough to not mention that little gastric outburst, or was too focused on the subject at hand to have noticed.

“Liara…” Shepard needed to choose her words carefully. She didn’t want to upset the heifer of an Asari before her with her line of questioning. And now, you’re telling me that everyone is super obese—”

“Hyper obese.”

“Well…” Liara began, cheeks turning a deep blue.

“We have no idea…” Not exactly the most satisfying answer, but hey, at least our benevolent machine goddess doesn’t seem to be at fault. Everyone else has just lost all self control.

1153113223132211.content:
Slightly less than six years and one hundred and twenty pounds ago...

"I really don't need this," Miranda sighed, parking the hover-car into a spot outside of a woman's military correctional facility and finishing her drink.

Not what Lawson needed when her clothes were getting snug for the first time in her life.

"Ugh, I look disgusting," she sighed at herself in the rear view mirror, although the face looking back at her was modelesque, , "but its not like its a date."

Lawson slid out of the car, gathered the empty bag with its burger wrapper, fry container and soda to put in a nearby wastebin and stood...after carefully sucking in her stomach and redoing her skirt button and belt. It wasn't that she had that big a gut, but the small skirt was made to tightly fit a woman without one at all, like Miranda had been when she'd bought it. And now that she had the start of one, getting into the skirt and staying in was a hassle.

Once her starter pooch was secured inside a wide belt, she clicked across the asphalt, tugging the skirt against her hips and thanking whatever God existed that at the end of the war, form fitting jumpsuits were no longer in vogue. Peace had brought a return to older fashions, ones somewhat more forgiving towards problem areas. For which Miranda, now in possession of one, was grateful.

As she entered the building, a blue glowing Reaper destroyer sailed slowly overhead. The brunette repressed a shudder, no matter how much good the "Shepherds" had done the last few months she'd always find them disturbing, and entered. Inside the recently rebuilt reception room was cool and calm, although a few sexist off duty marines here to pick friends up from the base jail gave her eyes and a wolf whistle. Miranda was used to them but still felt...well, first, slightly gratified that she was still getting them after gaining a whopping twenty pounds in three months. And extra annoyed that without her powers she couldn't flatten them anymore with a thought. Putting that aside, the tall woman approached the desk, finding a female MP manning it.

"I'm here to pick someone up," Miranda said, noticing the heavy gut the dark skinned woman had hanging over her gunbelt.

"What's your boyfriend's name," the MP said, their tone bored and a doughnut going into her mouth.

Miranda watched the pastry, drool building in her mouth. Three months of dieting since her biotics had failed was making her incredibly hungry and the sight of the guard, plump and chubby for a soldier, eating the donut without thought made the ex-Cerberus operative both envious and slightly...aroused. She put the last thought away, it was one that was all too common to her and best avoided. Such a disturbing and base fetish in a woman like herself was...undignified.

"Not my boyfriend thankfully," Miranda said, "Jacquelyn Nought."

The guard's eyebrows went up, "That psycho? Sheesh, hope you're ready for a fun six months."

"I'm sorry, six months? Isn't this just a pick up?" Miranda asked, hand pausing as she put it to her purse.

"Military court of justice ruling, for a violent dishonorable discharge they have to be remitted to someone willing to stand to for any material damage they commit," the bored guard said, sucking down a soda, "I'll need name and ID."

Miranda paused. She really didn't owe this person anything, certainly not putting already tight financial resources up for her behavior. But still, how many friends did she have? Did she even have any friends? Acquaintances even?

"Fine, I'll sign," Miranda sighed, pulling her billfold from her purse, a candy bar wrapper she shouldn't have eaten yesterday falling out.

It drifted to the floor, Miri's cheeks going red as she failed to catch it. Bending at the knee, she carefully bent to pick it up, feeling her skirt bite into her softening waist and squeeze at her spreading backside. A seam complained but nothing tore, although she was sure the waiting room got a great view of her bubble butt.

"Miranda Lawson?" the guard asked her, looking up from a rare official document of Miranda's that showed her real name.

"Yes, that's me," Miranda said, hoping she wouldn't get spit on.

"We oughta throw you in here instead, worse thing that psycho did was punch someone. Speciest collaborating bitch like you should have been hanged," the guard hissed, "I'll go get her. You deserve each other."

Miranda glared at the guard's ample backside as she disappeared around a door. Being ex-cerberus after that organizations downfall had been awful, her name had been outed across Alliance space by the Illusive Man as an operative after she'd defected, Miranda finding herself easily identifiable. After the paramilitary organization had gone mad, brainwashing people and attacking Citadel space during the War, her reputation had become mud and it was only through close cooperation with the Alliance and dear, dead Shepard that she'd avoided a jail cell. News of her father putting so many family resources into Cerberus and the brainwashing camps was just another nail in the coffin, giving people a personal reason to hate her.

Well, fuck them for that too. Without her help they'd all be dead, or Husks wishing they were. Instead they were alive, fat and happy, over eating Shepard provided nutrients.

So caught up in bitter thoughts was Miranda that she almost missed Jack being brought out. The "Psychotic Biotic" as she'd been nicknamed didn't look like a war hero who'd saved an academy of kids and killed hundreds of Cerberus agents and Husks. One eye was swollen from a punch, as was her lip and her once tight buzzcutt had grown out to cover her head tats.

She'd also gained, maybe sixty pounds.

Back on the Normandy, Jack had been stick thin. A biotic's gifts used calories, the greater the force used the greater the calories needed and Jack had been very, very strong. As a result she'd been skin and bones, with the appetite of a starving lion, shoveling trayfuls of food down at meal times, putting the large dishes the comparatively weaker biotic Miranda had been to shame.

But now of course, there were no biotics and those heavy appetites weren't being burned off anymore.

Jack's concave stomach had blown up into a real gut, one that dwarfed the little starter belly Miranda sucked in all the time. It hung over a tight pair of fatigue pants, shamelessly exposed in a vest Miranda wasn't sure Jack could button due to the sprawl of love handles and the bulge of her drooping belly. Above them, once flat breasts had shot out to DDs, pressing against a crop top and unsupported by a bra. The owner of a large bust, once DD but now E, herself, Mirana knew how uncomfortable that must be on Jack's back. Even her face, aside from the signs of a fight, had rounded out, what was lean and angular now cherubic.

"Have to say Cheerleader, never thought I'd be happy seeing you," Jack grumbled as her cuffs were taken off and she was given a small duffel bag of possessions, "and kinda surprised you came."

"...I'll say I am too," Miranda said, tearing eyes off the former Subject Zero's paunch, "and leave it there. Shall we?"

Outside, Jack blinked at the sun, whistling to see two Shepard dreadnoughts using mass effect tractor beams to sift rubble and rapidly build new, effecient arcologies on the horizon. Miranda caught her grasping a fist, the old gesture Jack had used to trigger her powers, but of course, now nothing came of it.

"Coming?" Miranda asked, pulling the rental keys from her purse.

"Wait, with you?" Jack tore her eyes away from the sight, "Thanks for bailing me out Cheerleader, but sheesh. No need to roll out the full welcome wagon."

"Do you have any place better to go? And besides, I had to sign a thing saying I was responsible for you, if you go break something they'll probably put me in jail over it. Not that they need much excuse," Miranda sniffed.

"Doubt I'm up much to breaking anything," the released prisoner grunted, patting her gut, "not without powers or a lot of gym time."

"Well, I might be able to help. Or at least I'm trying to," the ex-operative said, "and I"m sure you'd find a way even if you were down to one arm and an eyeball."

"Heh, you're probably right," Jack snorted, following the much taller woman.

Miranda couldn't help but note that Jack had trouble keeping up with her. Jack couldn't help but note Miranda's always well displayed butt had gotten a lot bigger.

"Thanks by the way, for not mentioning it. Its not like I give a fuck, but I'd kinda expected something catty. The warden gives us six squares a day and more for good behavior," Jack said, trying to get the seat belt done across her stomach.

"Well, you're not the only one," Miranda admitted, wincing as her waistband cut into her waist, 'losing the biotic surplus has done a number on me."

"Well, you carry less and better," the much more overweight Jack said, "if it weren't for your tits you could probably see your feet."

"I'm sure," Miranda sighed,"do you have any plan or anything? I know that you don't have anyone..."

"Well, I was planning on staying in the military. But then you know, no more biotic goddess powers and suddenly I'm just a bunch of authority problems. And then I punched the officer who disbanded my biotic unit into a coma. He shouldn't have said I was useless," Jack shrugged, "or at least done it in punching range."

Miranda chuckled, for the first time at one of Jack's jokes, "Well, in that case, maybe you can help me. I'm trying to solve a mystery and could use your help..."

Why we lost our powers: Why exactly our biotic powers shut off. With both of us to use as a baseline, we might find some answers.

What happened to Shepard Shepard disappeared when she fired the conduit, is she still in those things?

I want us to get our biotic powers back,” Miranda told her.

“I inherited a lot of bombed out factories on earth, whatever off planet assets weren’t used by Cerberus and blown up were seized by the military. Most of the company accounts were sued and the only thing I have are a single automated factory freighter, an outdated research space station attached to said freighter and settlement rights to an uninhabitable rock,” Miranda explained swiftly, “I could barely afford to come here and sign some papers giving me custody of my sister, who’s also my only research assistant at the moment. So room, board and a bigger shirt are about what I can pay you tubby.”

For a moment Jack looked at Miri, then broke into harsh laughter.

“Holy shit, Miranda’s poor! Oh fuck, I’ll stick around just for that,” Jack laughed, flabby gut bouncing up and down.

“I’m not poor I’m just...not rich. If I can cure the biotic issue then just the Asari will pay me enough to rebuild,” Miranda said defensively.

“Sure, sure,” Jack giggled, setting down as the rental car approached the space elevator.

For a moment there was silence, then the gurgle of Jack’s belly cut in.

“I, uh, hate to ask it of you after bailing me out, but well, they let me out just before lunch,” Jack said after a moment of garbage disposal groaning, gesturing at a string of recently rebuilt fast food places.

“...fine,” Miranda sighed, pulling into one, “do you want a salad?”

“A salad? Fuck no, I want a real burger,” Jack huffed, “Listen, I told you I don’t care about getting fat. A burger, fries and a shake. Pretty please.”

Miranda pulled up to the menu, the fast food looking succulent to her. She knew she really didn’t need it, she’d already had one fast food meal that day, for craps sake! And that she was about to pop out of this skirt and that high fashion didn’t grow on trees.

But she ordered herself a burger anyway. The stress was getting to her, was how she justified it. The sight of the cashier, young woman with a new double chin and a muffin top pouring over her pants, made Miranda feel a bit of stress and a bit of that horrible, undignified lust for the soft, jiggly and overweight that kept cropping up. She lingered getting her receipt, swallowing excess drool.

The two ex-biotics ate quickly, although Miranda felt herself bloat and squirmed in her seat as her stomach pressed even tighter into her skirt. It was going to be hours of waiting in the commercial space elevator and then catching a shuttle back to her own ship/home/lab and all she wanted was to undo this torture device. The sound of a zipper stirred her attention, she saw that Jack had fully undone her pants to let her gut breathe!

"What? Hey, I'm a chubby girl now and the fucking warden wouldn't give us more clothes, the fucking creep," Jack huffed, rubbing the soft roll of fat right above her undies, "don't tell me you don't want to do the same. I can see you swelling against your skirt."

"Please, like I'd ever," Miranda responded, tearing her eyes away from Jack and getting back on the road.

....

"But you still haven't told me how any of this happened!" Shepard objected back in the present in Miranda's office.

"Look its a long story, okay," Miranda said, a delivery robot appearing at the door to put a steaming extra large meat lovers on her desk, "and if you keep interupting me it'll be longer, which will mean I'll have to get more snacks which means I'll get fatter! Do you want that on your conscience!?"

"I've already got a galaxy of fat girls on my conscience, okay?" Shepard said, slightly defensively, "and you're one to talk."

"...Touche," Miranda said, folding up an immense slice, "So, where was I..."

....

"I'm gonna hit the shower and go to bed," Oriana yawned from the other side of the lab, stars showing out of the laboratory freighters port hole.

"Come on, one more test," Miranda pleaded, pushing her hair back, "then lets hit the gym."

"Miri, it hasn't gone anywhere for...ever," her sister sighed, "I'm sorry but I don't think this is going to work."

Oriana Lawson was Miranda's twin. Identical save for being much younger, an actual college age instead of just youthful like Miranda did. Like her, she'd been created from a cocktail of scientists, models and athletes to be a perfect woman in a rather creepy experiment, with Ori created as a back up in case Miranda failed.

"Ori, this is going to work, alright? Something will at least," Miranda told her, putting a hand on her sisters shoulder and squeezing, feeling how soft it was.

Miranda had always been pushed to be the best at everything: sports, scholastics, martial arts, extra curriculars. She'd been all state every year in high school in multiple sports, earned her first black belt by fifteen, valedictorian and everything else. It had left her stressed and feeling inadequate, when she'd fled her father's custody with the then infant Oriana in tow, she'd made sure her sister was adopted by a loving family. As a result, Ori had grown up softer and sweeter.

She didn't have Miranda's bitter edge, she was sweet and a bit of a nerd, slightly soft. Not a lot, but all of Miranda's exercise had left her hard and muscled, with a four pack right before her powers had vanished, where Ori had always had a hint of a stomach and soft cheeks. And where appearance focused Miranda had been exercising hard and vainly attempting to diet still, Oriana had been comfort eating the loss of her adopted family and ending of her normal life. As a result, where looking at her sister had once been like looking in a mirror (mostly) now it was like looking at the ghost of Christmas future. If and when Miranda hit 200 lbs she'd look just like her sister, after all, Ori had looked just like Miranda now did at 163.

For a while, her sister had carried her well, height and curves absorbing some gain but Ori was now in full fat girl territory. Ori's patrician features hid under puffy cheeks and a new chin. Her boobs were getting too big for her frame, full F cups that needed a bigger bra, while her once tapered waist was a full on beer gut hanging from under a too small shirt. Well rounded hips were getting sloppy, saddle bags breaking out and spreading them wide, hinting at a pear shape to come.

"That's what you always say," Ori grunted, "I just, I want to be able to go back to a regular school, the news said colleges are going to start up again. I want to be around people."

"And if your real identity gets out? We know what our sperm donor father did and so does everyone else," Miranda reminded her, "we need to clear our good name first

"yeah well...fuck," the fat Lawson groaned, "I'm going to bed. I've done too much tonight, have fun working out"

She walked off, hefty butt jiggling. Miranda shook her head, restarting the blood sample tests and letting that Ori had skipped two weeks of gym sessions go. She had enough to worry about, like the exhaustion of the blood tests. Both Lawson sisters and Jack had both donated a lot over the past couple of months, Miranda looking at the tiny fragments of naturally generated Element Zero that had gone dormant in their blood streams. She'd put them through a wringer of tests but found nothing was reactivating them.

It was frustrating, more frustrating than gaining another 11 pounds. None of her formal clothes would fit she guessed, this latest gain having put too much jiggle across her ass to risk her skirt. They hung up in her closet, while she clad her body in leggings and workout gear in the vain hopes of burning off enough weight to fit into them again. Instead the work out gear was getting tight, a sliver of belly starting to show between shirt and pants.

"Maybe she's right, maybe this is crazy," Miranda sighed, rubbing her eyes, "maybe nothing will restore our reputation...maybe..."

Maybe I need a snack... Lawson shouldn't, she was on a diet and still gaining weight. But a midnight snack or two might solve her issues.

Maybe I need a swim... A workout sounded good, the only problem was cardio was getting problematic with borderline F cups swinging around. Luckily the lab had lap pools.

Miranda Lawson blinked her gorgeous blue eyes, trying to clear the remnants of sleep and a hang over from them at the impossible sight.

The two identical brunettes flickered, memetic nanite holograms altering into Jack and Shepard. A thin Jack, totally fatless and bald, covered in tattoos, and a Shepard in the flesh, a tall and buff redhead with freckles down her face and into her ample cleavage.

"Yes, but you hate surrogate bots," Miranda pointed out to her wife, "you always said you felt like a freak with your consciousness in them!"

Jack shrugged robotic shoulders, "That's true. But the other times I've worn them, I've been surrounded by normal sized people, you know, regular eight or nine hundred pounders. They always look down on you for using a bot, like they think you think you're better than them. But..."

Lawson corp had created Surrogate bots using the spy android technology Cerberus had used to infiltrate Mars and had later been used to house EDI's consciousness. By wearing a 3-D headset and being submerged in a soft, oxygenated gel, the wearer's body slumbered while their consciousness lived in a body stronger, faster and tougher than any human had ever been. They'd been Miranda's way of letting a society trapped in their own obesity have strength and mobility without doing the impossible of dieting and exercising. While there'd been an initial burst of popularity among those eager to regain mobility, the fact that the Bots couldn't eat or taste (and thus couldn't experience the gleeful sensation of being painfully stuffed all humans now strived for daily) was a major turn off. Now they were used only by the Alliance's handful of police/military personnel, weird sexual deviants who liked sex between slim people and those who, like Jack, had grown so immense they needed to be put on starvation diets to drop weight before even Lawson's rehabilitative technology was unable to keep the dreadful consequences of their obesity from coming due. It was a good bet that any person with a singular chin seen in Alliance Space was actually a bot, Miranda had been accused of using one repeatedly due to her ability to stay mobile through good genetics, self denial, moderate exercise and regular cosmetic treatments. People generally disliked those who weren't always eating or stuck in a hover chair.

"Well, now when its just two skinny girls and you," Jack smiled, the surrogate bot's skin flickering back to an image of a lean, fit Miranda, "and I really feel better than you now that the 'real' me is suspended in a gel tank dieting off some of that ton plus of blubber I've got. Looking at what you used to have, all this hard muscle, these big firm breasts....did those doughnuts you sneak every morning you think I don't know about, do they really feel better than having abs?"

She punctuated the question by squishing Miranda's flabby, chubby gut.

"And your tits Miri," Shepard smiled, the machine goddess turning into a copy of Miranda again, "they're so perky and sensitive, while you've gotten loose and droopy!"

Shepard's fingers bounced under the bottoms of Miranda's drooping H cups, tapping against the stretched nipples.

"What...oohhhh...what are you doing?" the real Miri moaned.

"We're gonna bulk you up Miranda,' Jack's bot responded, pressing her hard abs into Miranda's plush body, "we're gonna fatten you up like you've helped fattened the whole galaxy up. You're eating for four now and we're gonna turn you into a big, fat, wobbly mommy."

"You're gonna have daughters to feed, so we've gotta swell your boobs up to handle that," Shepard's bot said, rubbing her soft, firm simulated breasts into Miranda's face, "great swollen tits that go down to your knees and just won't stop leaking."

Her face being rubbed in her current chubbiness was already driving Miranda wild with lust. The thought of being fattened up into yet another immobile hulk and of getting pregnant at last...

"Here, drink up. you're probably over heated," Miranda said, handing her the water and feeling the tug of her own bikini, "ugh, this is my fault thinking you could handle a walk."

Miranda blushed at that, patting her returned FUPA, "Well, when I get home its back on a diet. And you on the other hand are getting a hover chair, R&D have really comfy models that'll take care of all of your needs."

"Ha, you realize I'll blump up in one of those, walking is the only exercise I can sort of do!" the quarter tonner laughed.

11531132231322111211112.title: Miranda and Miranda! What!?

115311322313221112111122.content:
"Why do I have to do this again?" Miranda whined from inside the bathroom.

"Again, I'm sorry we have to bring you in but we need your thumb print on the signing for the hover chair deal," Shepard explained, her surrogate-bot body's high heels tapping as she waited for Miranda to finally be ready, "Liara understands you being pregnant but her board of directors is insisting on meeting in person so it has to be at the office."

"I...sure I understand," panted breath came from the bathroom, "I'm just having a bit of trouble with the clothes..."

Shepard rolled artificial eyes and put her hands on her hologram muscle hips in agitation. Miranda's public image as the most competent, caring person in the galaxy wasn't surviving being waited on hand and foot while eating for four. Once punctual and energetic, the brunette hadn't gotten up before noon in a month and that was only because of Shepard bringing her breakfast in bed. It had taken a great deal of prodding from both Shepard and Jack's robotic fingers to get Miranda up and about at the ungodly early hour of 10 am.

"Is she still in there?" Jack asked, her surrogate bot body walking up and easily holding a tray with half a dozen snack bags on it, "What the hell is she doing? We need to get airborne!"

"She's having trouble with the clothes," Shepard sighed, throwing a lock of hard light black hair over her shoulder.

Both machine goddess and immobile obese woman were wearing the surrogate suits and both had kept the robotic bodies set to replicate Miranda's long lost muscles. Both were essentially naked, wearing at best a thong and letting their tall, lean, curvaceous bodies be on full display to better rub the real Miranda's nose into what she was losing.

"The clothes? We measured her yesterday and put plenty of bulge room into the synthesizer, she shouldn't be having trouble with them for being too tight," Jack sneered, knocking on the door, "Miri, what's going on? Are they too tight? Did your boobs get bigger in your sleep again?"

"No, no I...the clothes are just a little complicated," Miranda whined from the other side of the door.

"Complicated? Miranda its a skirt, a shirt and a blouse, what's the problem?" Jack hissed.

"There's like...a lot of buttons!" the brunette whined, "and I haven't worn clothes in a while!"

"Fine, we're coming in,"Shepard said, opening the door.

When this had began, Miranda could still be corsetted down enough to look like she wasn't overweight. But after four months of eating for four, being waited on hand and foot and thrice dayly stuff sessions with her only exercise be walking around an infinity pool for an hour a day, the perfect woman had inflated.

Miri's face had gone from being just a little soft to having plush chipmunk cheeks and the start of a third chin, her cherubic features flush with embarrassment. Although only half way through the pregnancy, Miranda had popped early due to having triplets and her stomach was growing fast, pink and purple stretch marks rippling out across her new largest feature, a taut dome under a lot of soft fat. Her breasts weren't leaking yet but she'd start any day, Miranda's pendulous knockers had swollen into Ls, the false perkiness of bearing so much milk making them round again at the cost of a forest of stretch marks and immense sagging once she was done nursing. Stuffed with milk she might be, but Miri was also quite a bit fatter, her arms had been mostly slim but now they were chubby and wobbly, ditto for her legs which now had heavy cankles and swollen feet. One didn't have to be an omnipotent machine goddess to know that this pale fatass was over three hundred pounds.

"Damn it Miri," Jack sighed, taking in her wife.

"Its not that bad...," Miranda pouted from in front of the mirror, fat, clumsy fingers tugging at her buttons.

Expectant mothers had often complained of feeling mentally foggy, forgetful, ditzy and to be frank a little bit dumber when pregnant. The brilliant CEO hadn't expected this to happen to her, but the immense hormonal surges of having triplets (plus the treatment that had enabled her pregnancy to begin with) had ground her razor whit dull. It was all temporary but Miranda had gone from near genius to a complete airhead in a hundred days.

As exemplified by how Miranda had managed to button her blouse into her skirt and her skirt into bra while attempting to button her shirt. Somehow she'd managed not to get a single button properly placed!

"Its' just been a bit since I got dressed," the usually nude pregnant woman blushed.

The shepard piloted surrogate bot reached out and gave the fat woman's cheek a light pinch, "There there Miri, I know its upsetting, but why don't you let Jack and I take over for you. After all, buttons are complicated..."

"Good point Jane, you button, I'll feed," Jack's bot smiled, beginning to feed the always hungry Miranda while Shepard got the mess of buttons undone, "after all, we can't be late to our big meeting with the..."
115311322313221112111122.title: Four months.

1153113223132211121111221.content:
"Asari. The Blueberries are in pretty desperate need of your our biotic restoration therapies," Jack pointed out, using a hankerchief to wipe syrup from Miri's fourth chin, "you just need to put your thumb print on the dotted line and you'll be even richer. Then you can come back here and crash on the couch until its time to give birth."

"Oh...okay," Miranda said, eyes locked onto the remaining bananna, strawberry, blueberry chocolate hcip pancakes and thinking only of getting more food into her belly.

"And its a good thing to, this skirt's already about to blow," Shepard reported with a gentle prod into Miranda's dome of a baby belly, "hate to tell you this Miri but you're letting yourself go to seed."

"I am...not," the weak willed CEO said as another pancake was slid, heavenly into her mouth.

"Yeah, that's why you're barely recognizable," Jack laughed, waving a hand at the simulcrum of Miri's old abs and feeding her another pancake, "girl, you've got more chin fat than you used to have boobs."

"It's...not that bad," Miranda defended through a full mouth.

Getting Miranda to the office was a sharp inversion of her old routine. In past days, the comparatively fit and strong Miranda had guided Jack's hover chair into the cargo bay of her customized military aircraft and then plopped her round buns into the pilot seat, trusting her fast reflexes and quick wits to pilot the vtol to the office. Without Jack's ton plus real body's weight slowing them down, the gravid CEO was instead taken to a stylish hovercar by way of recently installed conveyor belts that insured Miranda didn't have to walk more than a few hundred steps.
