12313.content: On a mission to stop a crazy cult, Liara is blasted with strange particles that cause her to slip into different universes every time she sleeps or falls unconscious. And for totally not contrived reasons, she's fatter every time she leaps into a new universe! Can she make it back to her old timeline before it's too late?
12313.title: Eezo Leap

choices: Liara wakes up the next morning in the medbay.
Liara wakes up the next morning in her lab.
Liara wakes up the next morning...in the commander's bed?!
123131.content:
"Would you watch where you're driving Shepard?" Ashley Williams griped. "You're going to knock us out before we get there!"

"Doing my best here," Jane Shepard grunted as she banked to avoid a steep crater. "Whoever said the Mako was an off-road vehicle was a lying piece of shit."

"We're getting close, the signal's less then a mile away now," Liara T'Soni informed the fire team. "There should be a facility somewhere nearby. Garrus, do you see anything?"

"Yeah, right ahead. Looks like an old alien ruin. No guards outside." Garrus ducked out of the turret, bracing himself to avoid smacking his face against the side of the vehicle as Shepard hit a nasty crater. "You'd think a Prothean artifact this close to Asari territory would be a lot more heavily guarded."

"It's a cult, not a professional military unit," Ashley suggested. "If these Sisters are as unhinged as Liara says they are, they may not be clear thinkers. Should be pretty easy."

"I think if there's anything Saren's taught us so far, it's that zealots who aren't clear thinkers can still be dangerous," Shepard said in a total deadpan as the Mako slid to a stop. "Alright, weapons hot, we're moving in."

The commander and her squadmates descended the ramp, weapons raised as they assessed the situation. Now that they were up close, they could see the facility hidden by a clever use of camouflage and signal jamming from anyone passing overhead. Perfect way to hide something so that you could only find it if you knew it was there. Taking point with Ashley, Shepard silently counted down to one before blowing the door in with a biotic punch.

"Intruders!" an Asari inside shrieked. "How dare you desecrate our-"

A hail of gunfire cut them off as the fireteam charged in, taking down the Asari cultists in the entrance. The team barely had a chance to relax though as more cultists swarmed in from the walkways overhead.

"Ash, Garrus, get to high ground and keep these guys occupied," Shepard ordered. "Liara, you're with me, we're going straight in."

Liara took a deep breath as her pistol cooled down, bracing herself for the fight. Under cover from the gunner and the sniper, the two biotics raised their energy shields and charged in, pistol fire bouncing against their defenses before they barged into the next room. Immediately, Liara felt a twinge of excitement at the unmistakable sight of a Prothean beacon standing tall and proudly in the center of the room, surrounded with computer banks and odd looking generators.

"Looks like it's under construction," Shepard quipped as the two approached the device.

"Unlikely. It looks like they've modified this to their own purposes." Liara approached the beacon and looked at the computer systems. "It's...hard to say from a glance. They appear to be using it in conjunction with element zero and...something else."

"Well hurry up and shut it off, whatever it is. Maybe we can-"

"Heretics!"

"SHIT!"

Liara didn't even have time to turn before Shepard was bioticly thrown into her, smashing her against one of the generators. As Shepard dashed into cover on instinct to return fire, she didn't notice Liara woozily standing at the console just as it started to kick on. The Asari blinked, catching words flashing across the screen rapidly.

[[//ERR//]] Chronojump interrup[[//ERR//]]
[[//ERR//]]ack cascade imminent [[//ERR//]]
[[//ERR//]] Quantum[[//ERR//]]rticle over[[//ERR//]]
[[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]]ange[[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]]
[[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]][[//ERR//]]

Electricity arced down from the beacon into the computer bank, and the tanks on either side of Liara burst open, the white particles within flying towards her as she was lifted into the air by a surge of energy. A billion Images rapidly flickered through her mind in a millisecond before the beacon exploded and Liara dropped onto the ground, her heart threatening to punch through her rib-cage. She was alive, that much she was sure of, but...something was off. And she felt...tired...

"...Liara?"

Liara blinked before looking up at Shepard's tired face, a fresh gash across her right cheek. It took her another moment to recognize the stale, sterile air of the Normandy's medical bay.

"Shepard...?"

"Hey blue, how you feeling? That was a nasty hit you took."

"I've been...better," Liara admitted before realizing Shepard still had her armor on. "I wasn't out for too long, was I?"

"No, just a few minutes or so," Dr. Chakwas said from across the room as she tended to William's wounds. "You might have a mild concussion, I hope you don't mind me keeping you overnight. Ashley, stop squirming, you're making this difficult."

"Yes doctor, I know," Ashley grumbled as the doctor applied a gel to a nasty burn on his arm. "Shame we lost the beacon, would've made our jobs easier. Do you think it can be salvaged?"

"Unlikely, but it's worth a try. Might depend on how it was modified," Liara considered, then winced as her head throbbed painfully. "Ohhh..."

"I'd better go inform the council, then," Shepard said, getting to her feet and dusting herself off. "You two get some rest, you fought like hell out there."

"Two?" Liara repeated, looking over at Ashley. "What about Garrus, wasn't he-"

"What about me?" The automatic door slid closed behind Garrus Vakarian as he strode into the medbay, looking surprisingly healthy.

"Er...he was our backup in that fight," Liara remembered. "You had him take the high ground with Ashley while-"

"I've been on the ship since you three dropped in the Mako," Garrus interrupted. "Unless there's another Turian running around I don't know about." The three other squad members laughed at this while Liara sat there feeling puzzled.

"You took a bad hit, Liara. Your focus might be a little skewed as a result," Chakwas interjected helpfully. Liara wanted to agree, but she couldn't quite silence that nagging feeling in the back of her head telling her something was wrong.

"Maybe that rest will be good then," the Asari admitted.
123131.title: A Hop, Skip, and a Waddle!

choices: Liara researches the beacon to find out what it was used for.
Liara interrogates one of her squadmates from the assignment.
Liara goes straight to the commander.
1231311.content:
Not yet used to the ambience of the ship, Liara had a tough time falling asleep. When she did finally close her eyes, it only felt like she sat there for a few minutes before she glanced at the clock on the far end of the room, indicating a full night had passed. Groaning, Liara weighed the options of getting up now or trying to catch an extra hour or two. Her head still ached, maybe...

"Good morning, Miss T'Soni," Dr. Chakwas announced as she entered the medical bay with two steaming beverages in her hand. "I see you're up and about nice and early. Any trouble sleeping?"

"A little bit," Liara downplayed. "Just a weird nightmare. Felt like I was flying through my old memories as they changed around me."

"Sounds poetic," Chakwas said with a light chuckle.

"Only way I can really describe it. Plus I have to get some use out of my lit minor." The two of them laughed as Liara sat up, rubbing her forehead.

"I hope you don't mind that I got a coffee for you," Dr. Chakwas said as she handed one of the cups to her patient. "I'd usually be stricter in terms of your diet but I think a beacon exploding grants you a bit of respite."

"Diet?" Liara asked, raising an eyebrow as she pulled the covers off of her and spun her legs around to get up. "What-"

Liara's voice caught in her throat as she looked down and saw her slim, toned abdomen was gone. In its place was a chubby little belly, hanging just over the edge of her waistband. Liara couldn't stop herself from reaching up and poking it, letting a finger sink slightly into the pliable fat before running a hand down her leg to feel how there was a slight thickness to them as well.

"You've been making some great progress, dear," Chakwas said, placing an encouraging hand on Liara's shoulder. "You're not the only one to get a little heavy from a sedentary job. You'll shed those pounds in no time."

Liara nodded mutely, realizing that Chakwas' hair was a vibrant brown color instead of its usual silver. The door suddenly shot open and the Asari quickly rushed to cover herself up as an unfamiliar human in an Alliance beret entered, clutching his arm.

"Hey Doc, I think I sprained something trying to help Wrex move his things." The Alliance soldier then suddenly noticed Liara was there and gulped, standing a little straighter and changing his tone to sound more mature and impressive. "I...I sure I'm not too injured, but it can't hurt to be cautious!" The happy-go-lucky, pleading look in his eyes made Liara feel bad about not knowing who this was.

"Here, let me see," Chakwas said, going over to take his arm and give it a cursory once over. "Dear me," she concluded, clicking her tongue in disappointment. "Liara, could you inform the commander that Jenkins needs to have his arm amputated?"

"What?" Liara and the man, Jenkins apparently, said together.

"I'm only joking," Chakwas smiled. "Your arm looks fine, I'll give you some medication for the aches and you'll be fine by the day's end."

"Thanks Doc," Jenkins said. "I was worried, I didn't want to miss out on any of the action. After Eden Prime, I've been wanting to get back at those Geth any chance I get!"

"Just don't rush in and get yourself killed. I can't treat that with some pain medicine and bed rest."

Avoiding the conversation, Liara slipped out of the room after slipping into her proper clothes, folded neatly on the bed next to her. Something was wrong, she quickly realized. There were a lot of things that weren't the way they're supposed to be. Some more then others, Liara thought as she felt her new body wobble just slightly after a heavy footstep. Either way, something was seriously wrong, and her gut feeling told her it had something to do with that beacon.

Her next course of action, then, was obvious...
1231311.title: The Next Time...

choices: ...an unfamiliar Salarian, who introduced himself as 'Mordin'.
...another Asari, who wasn't exactly svelte herself.
12313112.content:
Liara was flying blind here. Her best guess for why things were suddenly different was the effects of a mind-altering chemical or biotic power, both of which had easily been ruled out by a quick medical exam. With barely anything to work with, she'd need to start right at the beginning and find out what, if anything, happened when they were fighting that mission. Ashley was away on a mission with the commander at the moment, so she turned to the other squadmate on the ship for his input.

"So there was no evidence of any sort of chemical attack?" Liara repeated, scrolling through her datapad.

"Not that I'm aware of," Garrus recalled, not turning away from the launcher he was maintaining. Evidently this Garrus was more into the heavy weapons then the hi-tech sniping, even having a bit more muscle on him underneath his blue and green armor. Did it have green before? No, definitely blue, Liara told herself. Don't forget the way things are supposed to be.

"And no biotic fields of unusual strength or makeup?" Liara continued, pulling herself away from her own thoughts.

"Not particularly," the Turian answered, snapping the last piece of the launcher into place. "Pretty standard stuff, walk in and burn the place to the ground. No more cultists, no more beacons and...well, never mind that."

Liara perked up. "Never mind what?"

"Nothing, it's just some garbage pseudoscience," Garrus said, making for the weapons rack to store the launcher. "Way too outlandish."

"Try me."

Garrus sighed before dropping onto the bench, with Liara sitting down across from him. "Well, it's something I saw the console flash at me before it cooked off...something about Chrozo saturation."


"Chrozo?" Liara repated, sounding out the word quietly once more. "I...never heard of that before."

"I only know about it because one of my co-workers back and C-Sec was a conspiracy nut who wouldn't shut up about it," Garrus said, rolling his eyes at the thought of said co-worker. "It's supposed to be a variation of Element zero that allows time travel."

Liara's datapad dropped out of her fingers and clattered to the floor. "I...time travel? But that's..."

"Impossible, I know. Element zero defies a lot of physics, but I know it can't do that," Garrus went on. "Like I said, garbage pseudoscience at best." Shaking his head, the Turian got up and strode out of the armory with a casual goodbye as Liara stayed motionless, the gears in her head turning fast. It was pseudoscience of course, even element zero wouldn't let you time travel, but if those cultists were trying to play around with eezo to manipulate reality on that scale...

Liara rushed back into her lab, only vaguely aware of how the extra weight on her thighs wobbled very slightly with a new hypothesis; the explosion did something to her that a standard medical practice wouldn't be able to find. Something that was doing serious things to her perception of reality. It'd be a doozy to prove, considering that she was trying to find something undetectable, but she had an ace up her sleeve. The easiest way to prove something was affecting her would be to compare it to something else that had to be effected; the parts of the beacon.

No stone was left unturned as Liara locked herself in her lab the rest of the day, the remains of the Cultist's device stacked in the center of the room ready to be experimented on. Mineral tests, topographical studies, chemical components, biotic applicability, molecular displacement, Liara did every test she could think of twice over, well into the night. Her eyes began to droop as experiment after experiment just barely failed, hinting that there was an answer SOMEWHERE, that it was just inches away from discovery.

Tapping away at a holographic display, Liara's eyes glazed over as she poured over molecular displays, looking like stars set against a night sky. The dull hum of the Normandy's engines echoed faintly in the background. Letting out a little yawn, Liara longed for her bunk on the other end of the ship, sleep starting to take her.

"Wait a minute..." Liara recalled. "Sleep..." Clicking rapidly, she brought up the particle simulation that she made hours ago to guess at the effects of Chrozo on certain materials. Punching in some numbers to simulate a background noise of brain functions, something that would affect these particles if they were affecting her, she turned down the background noise to simulate reduced neuron. Immediately the simulation sprang to life, the particles dancing and flowing like river water.

"That means..." the Asari concluded, pausing to stifle a yawn. "That...they might activate when...I...fall unconscious...or...fall..."

Liara's head slipped off of her hand as she swayed, her mental energy tapped out as her body demanded rest. "If ah fall..." she slurred as her head drifted gently onto the table, her eyes closed. "Fall...asleep..."

---

The next morning, as the ship corrected course to avoid an asteroid field, the higher pitch of the engine was enough to disturb Liara as she rested on her desk. Opening her eyes, she felt a twinge in her neck from the uncomfortable position she fell asleep in and sat up, rubbing the sore spot. Still, she was in a good mood, remembering the piece of the puzzle she found last night.

"What's that human expression again Faera?" Liara asked the colorful avian creature perched on the end of her desk. "Burning the midnight oil?" She let out a soft chuckle, then jumped in her chair when the bird squawked in response, unfolding two sets of wings and flapping about.

"Since when did I have...?" Liara began, before her eyes widened in shock when she realized the desk was facing the opposite way towards the window, and the lab was slightly larger with a much different set of equipment. Right, that puzzle piece was that things would change when she lost consciousness...or fell asleep. "Well at least I keep my memory," she reassured herself, trying to take stock of any other odd changes, like the different setup of her holokeyboard, the empty plate or two off to the side, the faint taste of chocolate still on her lips.

Looking down, Liara saw probably the most unwelcome change of all. Her belly from the previous day had not gone away; in fact, it was quite a bit bigger, sitting comfortably in her lap. Her skintight outfit, now a slick shade of purple, was even more skintight and lovingly displayed every roll and curve of it as well. Scrambling to her feet, she felt the telltale wobble of her thighs and the brush of the chair arms against her hips, even the slight jiggle of her arms as she reached up to feel her face, her plump cheeks and chin rounding her out. It was bad enough that she was experiencing these reality shifts, Liara vented to herself, but why did she have to be getting fatter in every one?

"Well hopefully this goes away in the next jump," Liara reasoned. "Or better yet, I'll solve this issue before the day's out. What do you think Faera?"

The bird squawked again and flew over to perch on her shoulder. Liara took that as a sign of encouragement and stroked the creature underneath its beak.

"Right, if these Chrozo particles are a thing," Liara reasoned. "I'll need to find where they're situated on my person. Maybe Chakwas knows something."

Waddling out of the lab, Liara meandered through the ship, trying to pick out any familiar landmarks so she wouldn't get lost. It still seemed to be early in the day for humans, so she didn't see any signs of life on the way to the medical bay.

"Doctor?" Liara called out. "I need your help with something!"

"Yes,who is it?" came a much different voice than Dr. Chakwas from the other side of the room. "Oh, Miss T'Soni, what a surprise!"

As they approached with their electronic clipboard in hand, Liara tried to hide her confusion at seeing the doctor was...
12313112.title: Ask Questions, Get Information

123132.content: While on an archeological visit to the Prothean ruins on Therum, Liara accidentally activates a security measure, not only trapping her, but making sure she wont be going anywhere...
123132.title: Security Alert

choices: Option 2: That's What Friends Are For
1231321.content:
Liara's going to be on the receiving end of some weird and quirky Prothean tech for sure, but is it going to be her first time dealing with something like this?

Option 1: Yes, Liara's going to be trapped by the Prothean force field when the Geth attack the planet, just like in the game. Can Shepard can find Liara in time before she'll need to be towed out by the Mako?

Option 2: Nope, Liara has returned to the ruins after joining Shepard's crew, looking for clues about what Saren is up to. Hopefully if something happens her new crewmates can help her out...if they don't get stuck in the same predicament she will be in.

Option 3: Definitely. In fact, it's Liara's first expedition as a newly graduated archeologist, years before she'll meet Commander Shepard. Who knows how such an experience will...ahem...change her?
1231321.title: All About Timing

12313213.content:
Therum probably wasn't anyone's first choice for an archeological expedition. Sure it had those ever valuable Prothean ruins, but after the corporations, the looting, and the volcanic activity Therum produced little more then raw metals and toxic smog. Even total novices just starting out on real field work could do better then this lava-encrusted wasteland.

On the other hand, with living workers being phased out in favor of machine labor and the planet being fairly distant from the more developed civilizations, it was perfect for aspiring archeologists who wanted a life away from the stifling politics of Asari Republics and to step out of their influential mother's shadow. Plus, Liara liked a good challenge, and the ruins promised plenty of them.

Her excitement at finally getting to properly study Prothean ruins in the field carried her through the first few days of tedium, where she mostly documented things she already knew that were barely worthy of being looted, but at last it was beginning to pay off. Working a battered old command console, the eighty-one year old Asari finished her scan at the bottom of an unused mine shaft and found the last few hours had paid off in spades.

"A Prothean operating system, I knew it!" Liara said allowed, to excited to merely think the words as she tapped more keys. "If I can just power it on..."

The floodlamps she set up flickered and dimmed as a power surge rippled through the room, culminating in an array of lights blinking to life on an ancient steel wall embedded in the rock face. Delicately flicking the controls back and forth, Liara used her knowledge of the Prothean language to input a binary command; "Open!"

Liara felt her heart soaring as the rock face shuddered and fell away as the steel door sluggishly lifted into the ceiling, unleashing a gust of ancient, stale air. Virtually untouched, probably for millennia, Liara concluded. Lifting up the nearest floodlamp with her biotics, she turned it to illuminate the interior and let out a small gasp.

Stasis pods. Liara counted at least half a dozen, situated in the small room as she stepped inside to explore. It had always been theorized that this was a technology the Protheans wielded, and this was definitive proof! Sure it wasn't the most planet-shattering discovery, but it was still quite something. Holding her omni-tool's light over the pods, Liara noticed on closer inspection they appeared to be damaged, not severely but they were all opened with the closing mechanisms in poor shape. A shame they weren't in use, maybe she could have met one. Liara laughed the idea off as ridiculous and started running a scan of the machine's inner workings.

Liara jumped as a tone suddenly sounded off, followed by a garbled alien language. Sparks flew across the faulty wiring hanging off the walls, but the young archeologist got such a rush from hearing the language that she didn't notice. Was that...was that the Prothean language? By the Goddess, she had to write this down! Turning to run out the entrance into the pod room, she-

*Fzzt*

Liara yelped as she ran right into an energy barrier, gently shocking her hands as she was thrown backwards. Shaking her head, the Asari stepped towards the entrance again, placing a hand on the barely visible barrier until the tingling sensation became too great. Some kind of forcefield, she realized. Another fascinating discovery, if it wasn't being used to trap her here.

Oh yeah, she was trapped now, Liara realized as she began to panic. This was definitely the only way out that wasn't through solid rock, and somehow she was stuck inside. She immediately turned to her omni-tool to try and send another command, but there was no signal; the command functions were sent through the console sitting on the other side of the shield.

Liara stumbled, her face paling as she placed a hand on her chest. Deep breaths, she told herself. You'll be fine. Someone's going to be down here to help, surely. Inputting some commands into her omni-tool again, she felt her heartrate steadying when it managed to at least send the general distress out. Someone will be here soon then, she thought to herself, and you can put all of this behind you. Imagine how fun this story will sound when they're awarding you for this incredible find! Just...try to breath slowly, the barrier is probably blocking oxygen.

As if in response, another shower of sparks rained from the walls, and this time the stasis pods all collectively hummed to life. Liara, who was starting to have trouble breathing in the contained room, immediately felt the effects as cool, healthy air filled her lungs again. Taking a moment to collect herself, the Asari started to feel much healthier then before, well-rested, energized. She even felt less hungry. Going by her limited knowledge of Prothean stasis pods, she figured their lifesaving functions must be affecting her since all of them were broken and open. Grateful for this stroke of good luck, Liara finally started to relax a bit, preparing to wait it out until any would-be rescuers arrived.

Then her hand brushed against something soft, and she looked down at something odd and new; a slight tummy pressing against her outfit. Liara frowned, unconsciously adjusting her suddenly tighter belt and having a sinking feeling that, maybe, the energy from six stasis pods might have an unintended side effect...

Choice 1: Liara is found and rescued quickly, but not before becoming a big girl in the process.

Choice 2: The rescuers are delayed, and Liara emerges from the Prothean prison super-sized.

Choice 3 The poor Asari is kept waiting for quite some time. You can guess what happens.
12313213.title: Her First Big Expedition

choices: Right away, when something strange begins happening to her mother.
In a few years, after Liara has significantly slimmed down.
To Commander Shepard when they and their crew meet her twenty years later.
123132133.content:
A Few Days Later...

In his ten years of experience in all things medical, Dr. Orkum Gozz had never encountered anything quite like this.

He had been on his way back from an uneventful scientific conference aboard his medical frigate when he received the urgent distress call from a human mining colony known as Therum, requesting aid. Expecting a large scale mining disaster, the good doctor had responded with diligence and began to prepare his team for immediate triage work when Dr. Gozz arrived to find the facility at Therum quite unharmed, and was told there was only one patient. He would have been incensed at such a pointless waste of time, until he saw the patient being lifted out on a construction hauler.

With the medical bay refitted for the new patient's condition and the ship speeding towards Thessia, the doctor had been doing his best to balance his scientific curiosity over the patient with his sympathy for the poor girl. It helped that she was quite intelligent herself and provided a lot of information on what had happened to her, which had done a lot to help him diagnose and treat the unusual condition. He was taking a break to meticulously record his notes in his office when the light streaking past his window faded as the ship ended its mass relay jump at their destination. Glancing upwards briefly, the doctor jumped as he saw the great silhouette of an Asari capital ship in front of the relay, seemingly waiting for his tiny ship's arrival.

"Sir, there's an Asari vessel contacting us," came the warning from his own vessel's pilot.

"Yes, I figured as much," Dr. Gozz griped. "What do they want?"

"It's an Asari Matriarch, she says she wants to see her daughter," the pilot relayed.

Dr. Gozz's pale red skin turned even paler. The patient neglected to mention her mother.

---

Omni-tool data at the ready, Dr. Gozz tried to look professional as the airlock hissed, signally the arrival of the powerful Matriarch on her shuttle. Feeling significantly exposed, surrounded only by two assistants, Dr. Gozz winced as the door opened an an older Asari strode out, her expression stoic and unflinching, dressed in an exceptionally expensive looking golden and white dress, flanked by a pair of those famed Asari commandos in jet black bodysuits.

"Er, welcome aboard, Matriarch Benezia," Dr. Gozz rattled off. "It's an honor, though I wish we were meeting under better circumstances."

"Where is my daughter?" Benezia demanded, coolly going over the doctor's greeting as promptly as she walked past him.

"Er, right this way and I'll explain," Dr. Gozz said, doing a skip and a run to catch up to her. "It's difficult to explain, but as best we can tell, there was an accident involving some Prothean medical technology."

"Is she-" Benezia started sharply before trailing off, her brisk pace slowing down. Gozz was taken aback at the look that he gave her; almost with a hint of pleading.

"Alive? Yes, she's going to be perfectly okay."

Matriarch Benezia let out a small breath, pausing to wipe a tear from her eye. "If I may ask," she continued, her speech hardening into that elegant tone again. "Why then the need for you to escort her home?"

This was the part Dr. Gozz was afraid of, and he had the slight suspicion that this Matriarch knew it. Feeling her eyes boring into him, he flipped through some data on his omni-tool to prepare himself.

"Well, she was bathed in an abundance of something similar to biotic energy with a high regenerative factor," the Salarian doctor began. "So she's actually perfectly healthy despite her...condition..."

"Condition?"

Dr. Gozz nodded. "Maybe you should see for yourself."

Following the doctor mutely to the medical bay, Matriarch Benezia's patience was starting to run low. She didn't enjoy being kept in the dark like this, especially in regards to her daughter. The two had a rather unpleasant fight before she left on her archeology adventure, and when she heard about her daughter being involved in an accident she feared that her fight would be the last memory she had of her. Say what you will about her, but she cared very much about her daughter, even to the point of blowing off a chance to invest in a human genetics corporation to rush to her side. She was not going to leave her daughter alone to suffer through this.

"This will be something of a shock," the Salarian doctor warned her as they approached the door.

"I know, just let me see her," Benezia demanded, trying to force the image of Liara lying half-dead in a hospital bed out of her mind. "Alestia, Orteri, wait for me at the ship, I want to be alone with her." The two commandos mutely obeyed and retreated as the Matriarch stood alone, waiting to see what had become of her daughter. Slowly the large doors slid open, and...

"...is this some sort of joke?"

Stepping inside, the room was immediately dominated by a large gelatinous shape, blue and freckled, lying on a large pile of sheets where a lot of the beds had been pushed aside to make room.

"I said I wanted to see my daughter!" Benezia repeated, rounding on the poor doctor, her eyes glowing with anger.

"M-mother?" came a deep but familiar voice.

Benezia turned back to the large blue blob as it wobbled slightly, her fury dissolving as she realized with mounting, incredulous horror what exactly this was. The drooping shape, the prominent cavernous belly button. Stepping closer, she saw on either side mounds of blubber that were once legs with chubby feet uselessly dangling in the air, then breasts bigger than a Krogan's hump drooping off to either side. An arm with massive bingo wings and a hand barely able to clench its sausage-like fingers stuck straight out, forcing Benezia to step around it.

"...Liara?"

Her daughter's face was unrecognizable, flooded with massive cheeks and a heaving second chin that sat on a bib of neck fat, but when she saw her eyes she knew. Liara didn't turn to face her, she probably couldn't between her meaty shoulders and face, but there was a twitch that may have been a smile.

"Hello...mother..."

Benezia stared at her daughter for what felt like ages, not knowing what to do. Scream? Run out? Faint? So many emotions seemed to war inside of her until at last she reached down to stroke her daughter's immensely fat cheek. Liara wobbled slightly as she started to giggle, and soon they were both laughing at the utter absurdity of the situation.

"Well, at least you're taking it well," Dr. Gozz timidly said. "This should be reversible, but there could be some lingering changes..."

"I've been...through...worse..." Liara huffed dismissively. "Just...get us...to Thessia..."

"Of course," the Salarian said as he made his escape, leaving the two alone. For a long time, they just sat there staring at one another, probably still trying to parse all of this out.

"Well..." Liara huffed at last. "This...will take some...getting used to..."

"Don't worry, Little Wing, we'll manage," Benezia said soothingly.

"Little...wing?" Liara repeated. "Not...anymore."

The two of them shared another bought of laughter, starting their long journey of getting back to normal in good spirits. It wasn't going to be easy, especially since those lingering changes that the doctor warned her about were going to become apparent...
123132133.title: That Only a Mother Could Love

1232.content:
Nothing added up.

Liara had been tracking down records and pathways from The Observer, an agent of the Shadow Broker, for months. Hacking terminals, tracing logs, even narrowing the list to 5 species, based on the information Nyxeris gave her.

But still, nothing added up. Liara had confronted dozens of potential suspects, subjecting them to various forms of biotic interrogation, but none were The Observer, or even had any information on them. As much as she hated to admit it, she was getting nowhere, and she desperately needed sleep.

“Liara? Are you alright?” Her assistant, Nyxeris asked.

“Yes, I’m fine,” Liara started, exhaustion clearly weighing her down, “I believe I have been awake for almost 40 hours and I’m nowhere close to finding The Observer. I’m going to get what sleep I can, but if you find anything, do not hesitate to wake me.”

“Of course,” Nyxeris bower her head, hiding the slight smirk across her lips.

“Thank you, Nyxeris, have a good evening,” Liara said as she began the walk to her apartment. She walked in and immediately fell into her bed like it was an old friend she hadn’t seen in years. Sleep took her immediately, but not pulling her mind away from The Observer, as she was tormented with dreams of the shapeless Observer capturing her and torturing her, subjecting her to all sorts of malicious and, sometimes strange, punishments.

Liara awoke in a cold sweat, thanking the Goddess that the experience of being forced into a toothpaste tube and squeezed out wasn’t real. She looked at the clock on the wall; 7:30, the first signs of light were peaking through her window. She’d somehow managed a full night’s sleep, and she’d never felt more refreshed.

After donning a fresh dress and performing her morning routine, Liara was about to head out with a renewed drive to catch The Observer, but her doorbell rang before she could. Liara was cautious while on Illium. Assassins and thugs, hell, even agents of the Shadow Broker could have just rung her doorbell. She readied a biotic blast and checked the camera outside. She was surprised to see...
1232.title: The Observer

choices: Her mind snaps back to her
12321.content:
“Nyxeris?” Liara spoke over the intercom, “What are you doing here?”

“I’ve discovered some information on The Observer, but we must talk inside, I believe I may have been tailed” Nyxeris responded in a hurried tone, and Liara was quick to unlock the door and let her assistant in. She locked the door behind her, keeping an eye on the security camera.

“Alright, what have you found?” Liara asked, too distracted to see Nyxeris aim a biotic blast at her.

“You’ll see, Liara.” Liara only had a split second to realize what was happening before she blacked out.

——————

Liara awoke with a start, noticing immediately that she was restrained. Not only that, but her mind was foggy as well, not letting her concentrate on her biotics.

“Nyxeris...” Liara snarled. Of course, The OBSERVER, the Shadow Broker sent Nyxeris to observe her, how stupid was that. Then again, what does that say about her.

“Don’t try to escape, you have a biotic damper on you,” Nyxeris postulated as she fiddled with a large device she had brought into Liara’s room, “This was a fun game, Liara, but you were getting a bit too close for comfort. And so, I’ve been authorized by the Shadow Broker to... neutralize you.”

Liara maintained her ground, however. “If you’re going to kill me, kill me, but just know that if you do, you will incur the wrath of-“

“Oh we know,” Nyxeris cut Liara off, “we know of the exploits of the great Commander Shepherd. If he came after our organization, we wouldn’t stand a chance.” Nyxeris raised her hand to Liara’s head, biotic energy surrounding it. “We have other ways to neutralize our enemies.” Nyxeris released the biotic energy and Liara felt an enormous mental assault on her brain.

“Biotic Suggestion; a technique they teach the agents of the Shadow Broker who are proficient in biotics, making the target extremely susceptible to suggestion.” Liara felt her mind being attacked, trying to force her way through it, but the damper on her biotechs made it impossible.

“Now then,” Nyxeris said, “you never found any information about The Observer or the Shadow Broker, and you do not know that I am The Observer. Is that clear?”

“Y-yes,” Liara didn’t have the strength to deny this, as she felt information leave her mind.

“Perfect, now,” she walked over to the device and grabbed a tube with a nozzle on it, “you will drink this, clear?”

Liara nodded as the tube was inserted into her mouth.

“This substance will attach to the fat in your body and multiply, making you too fat to fight, or even move. You will not stop this from happening. Is that clear?”

Liara’s mind raced, but she couldn’t help but nod her head.

“In fact,” Nyxeris had a devilish grin as she thought of a way to add icing to the cake,”...you’ve always wanted this, to be fat. It’s your fetish, you want to be a fat blob of an Asari. Is that clear?”

All other thoughts in Liara’s mind were eclipsed by this new desire; to be fat, to have pounds and pounds of beautiful blue blubber on her. Liara nodded her head.

“Perfect.” Nyxeris started the machine, and a milk-like substances ran through the tube and into Liara. It tasted like heaven. Even if she wanted to stop, which she didn’t, the pressure forced her to take gulp after gulp of the liquid. After a few moments, Liara felt something happening in her; she could feel herself gain weight. Her flat belly was starting to billow out, her modest breasts were growing, her trim thighs were thickening, her toned ass was plumping up. She moaned quietly at this feeling. It was everything she ever wanted, or so she thought.

Seconds passed and Liara’s belly was now a pot belly, straining the dress she had picked out. Her boobs had grown a full cup size by now, resembling grapefruits in size. She could feel mounds of back fat and side rolls growing under her bra. The most drastic change was below the waistline, however, as most of the plumping was happening in Liara’s thighs and ass. She could feel pound after pound going to her blue butt, making it grow and stretch the fabric of her dress. This also caused to dress to ride up, exposing her thickening thighs, which jiggled with every little movement. Liara could see her belly and thighs grow before her very eyes, and the sight elicited another light moan from the Asari.

‘Goddess, this is... amazing...,’ Liara thought, the only thing present in her mind being pleasure.

“You probably don’t need these anymore,” Nyxeris removed the restraints from Liara, and her hands immediately flew to her belly, squeezing and jiggling it as she felt it grow in her hands. She felt her back fat and her boobs, her plump thighs and ass, she even felt a double chin growing with every gulp. Soon, her belly plumped enough to break from her it’s fabric confines, spilling out into Liara’s lap. She was in ecstasy, until...
12321.title: Nyxeris

choices: A friend comes to help Liara with her...condition.
123212.content:
"And the Shadow Broker said you were a difficult target," Nyxeris taunted, joining Liara in rubbing her hands over her growing belly. "I must say, I'm quite disappointed with how easy you were to take down." Her gentle kneading elicited another moan from the gradually fattening Asari, and as she leaned closer, she didn't see the spark of life suddenly flare up in Liara's eyes, her willpower finally winning out against Nyxeris' manipulations.

Liara could hardly believe what was happening to her, but she managed to keep up the facade, groping her blossoming rolls of fat as best she could to keep Nyxeris at bay. She didn't like her odds in a biotic vs biotic brawl, since it took some effort to break her control, and judging by the tingle of arousal stirring in her loins, it hadn't entirely gone away; she'd need another option. She looked at the innocent coffee table just within her reach...

"You make such a cute little piggy," Nyxeris cooed, drawing herself so close to Liara's face that she could feel the heat of her breath. "Only good for eating and getting fatter and fatter..."

You wish, Liara thought as she nodded her head desperately, pretending to be lost to arousal. She didn't need to try too hard to fake as Nyxeris joyfully climbed onto her growing stomach, eliciting an erotic moan as her knees dug into her belly fat, along with a sharp metal crack from somewhere below Liara's massive backside. Nyxeris and Liara both realized a split second too late that the chair was not designed for the both of them at this point as gravity sent them crashing to the ground. Nyxeris bounced off of Liara's growing gut as she flopped heavily to the ground, while the top of Liara's dress exploded into scraps, followed by her rear end bursting through what was left of the lower half, revealing her lacy white panties and the bra failing to contain her melon-sized breasts. Springing into action, Liara reached underneath the coffee table and pulled out a plan black circular device, lobbing it across the room right at a surprised Nyxeris still getting to her feet.

Letting out a choke as the circle attached itself to her neck, she stumbled backwards as she tried to pry it off, getting her nails under the edge before it clicked, discharging a bolt of paralyzing electrical energy. Liara smiled as her body immediately froze in place like a statue, save for Nyxeris' eyes darting back and forth; that Batarian paralyzer had been money well spent, and would keep her captor turned captive nice and safe until she got what she wanted out of her. Maybe she would use that fattening device on HER, Liara thought with demented excitement.

Then karma struck; the paralyzer had frozen Nyxeris off-balance and when she fell over after a few seconds, she clipped the side of the device, snapping a lever off the valve attached to the hose. Liara watched with mounting dread as the hose bulged outwards at frightening speed; she tried to extract it from her mouth, but it wouldn't budge; Nyxeris had attached a strap to keep her from spitting it out. She let out a muffled and useless cry for help as the deluge flooded into her mouth, forcing her to swallow before she suffocated from the stuff. Falling on her back, Liara could do nothing but wobble as the fluid flowed into her so quickly it couldn't all turn to fat fast enough. Her belly bulged outwards like a massive balloon, creaking and bubbling as Liara swallowed and drank as quickly as she could, barely getting a breath in before more of it came. Growing increasingly lightheaded from lack of oxygen, Liara's last two thoughts was a deep fear she was going to burst...and a feeling of intense pleasure.

----------

A light belch escaped Liara's lips as she opened her bleary eyes. Her head was throbbing painfully, and something sticky was coating the bottom of her face. Groaning painfully, she tried to wipe at the substance on her lips but failed to even lift her hand from the ground; something heavy pinned her arms to the ground. She tried to move to sit up, but felt the same sensation of heaviness keeping her firmly on the ground. She could barely even turn her head from side to side, her face felt so swollen. It should have made her feel afraid or panicked, but Liara felt...content, laid back. Almost happy even, like she enjoyed a good meal and a well-deserved nap. She wondered what she was even doing here before she remembered that her assistant was secretly a mole for the Shadow Broker sent to neutralize her with a...fattening...machine....

Oh crap.

Liara tried again to lift up her hands, struggling to bring them into view. Her jaw dropped as she saw her fingers swollen to the size of sausages, barely able to move, and her padded palms and wrists being overtaken by her fat forearms, sacks of blue blubber wobbling with the slightest movement. Dropping her hand to her jaw, she ran a hand down to feel her chin...chins, the second fused into a huge ring of fat restricting her movement. Now fully aware of what happened, she realized her cheeks were so fat that they were somewhat blocking her line of sight. Lifting her head with immense difficulty, she saw past the two basketball-sized breasts flopping to either side of her flabby chest, and beyond that...a massive apron of a belly reaching up to her calves, making a cuff of fat around her bloated feet. She had to wiggle her chubby toes to be sure they belonged to her.

Nyxeris's machine hadn't just fattened her up. It turned her into a blob.

Anger redoubled her efforts to get to her feet, but even then it took her a good five minutes to get up, a combination of biotics and clever usage of furniture to lift her enormous rear end off the ground. Panting and sweating as she finished the arduous tag, Liara spotted what was left of her outfit in tatters on the floor, looking like discarded napkins compared to her immense size. Still catching her breath as sweat poured down her face, she looked at the doorway out of the office, knowing there was absolutely no way she could get out; not even a single one of her beanbag-chair-sized butt cheeks could fit through that door.

Even walking was a monumental task; Liara had to put every bit of effort she could into staying upright, wincing as she felt the floor tiles cracking under each footstep. Still, she kept pushing herself further and further until she came up to Nyxeris, still restrained inside the paralyzer. She didn't know if her assistant was awake in there or not, but she didn't care.

"You're going...gasp....to pay for this," Liara huffed. "You and your....wheeze....boss"
123212.title: Snap Back to Reality, Oh There Goes Gravity

choices: It is no time for sweets
12323.content:
A volus delivery man holding bouquet of red roses and a purple package.
"Delivery for ... bzz ... Liara T'Soni" proclaimed short alien, while holding out hands with gifts.

"Thank you Mister...?" replied bevilded asari, that certainly was not turn of events she was expecting. However could not allow herself to lose focus, she seen more ridiculous things than volus assassins. She took items and put them on small table near door and turn again to supposed assailant.

"Kon Narlan from EEzio..bzz...Ilum fastest and most reliable delivery service" said not unlike automat. Clearly he was used to replaying those words over and over again. At the same time he took holopad from his pouch.

"Please sign here to confirm receiving package" continued volus.

Liara forced herself to show her best professional smile and took holopad. SShe glanced on text apparently it was pre-paid delivery from anonymous sender. She filled all forms and gave device back to delivery man. "Is that all?"

"Yes...bzz..good night" replied volus and started walking away.

Asari keep watching until he disappeared behind the corner. She closed door and breathed out. The Observer must be getting to me if i react like that to a doorbell. She looked at gifts. Both roses and package seemed harmless, but in her situation she could not allow herself to become careless. She carefully took items and placed them on station capable of scanning and identifying dangerous substances and items. While machine was working she sat and ran quick search. Company was real enough, Liara herself used their services in the past and as she learned they indeed employed volus under name "Kon Narlan", ther details of delivery seemed to be correct as well, but there was no information about sender, even account which he used to pay for delivery was not listed.

That's suspicious, but then again not a lot of people likes to be linked to information brokers, even though they try to keep in your good graces She looked away from desktop and a scanner. While she was browsing machine finished scanning gifts.

"Let's see" Liara looked on device's screen. According to its reading roses were expensive, but besides that completely ordinary and available in most of better shops with flora.

Shepard liked roses She thought to herself with melancholy. She remembered how Commander had bought bouquet of those for her not too long after battle for Citadel. It was first time she saw those flowers and took liking to them as well. Elizabeth explained that roses were considered to be a symbol of love.

Asari smiled to herself briefly, but then scolded herself. Deal with possible danger first, ponder on past later.

According to machine, package didn't contain, explosives, traps or any substances that may be harmful to asari.

Better check it now and be sure than wait with it until morning decided Liara and began to cerrfuly unwrap package... only to reveal a box of chocolates and small slip of paper.A paper letter? Asari had not used paper for writing since millennia, nowadays only humans use it sometimes.

She took it and read:

"Dear Miss T'Soni

I apologize for secrecy, but due to your occupation questionable reputation I rather not be linked to your company.
At the same same time I simply must send those modest gifts to thank you for help with my problem and tell you that I hope that we meet again under more pleasant circumstances.

Yours sincerly
Adam Constantine


Adam Constantine... She remembered him. A middle age human male businessman that need help looking for few people that knew about his less than law abiding past. He was bit eccentric and left Earth not that long ago, what more he seemed to be enamored with her. Yes, expensive flowers and chocolates, it is something he would do.Unfortunately for you Mister Constantine, there is only one human for me

Now she inspected chocolates, they were vacuum sealed with no sign of tampering.. I recognise that brand. It's the same one Ashley got on her birthday from her sisters. t it is different type, more expensive one. Huh... Garrus got allergy after eating one of those on a dare, he didn't leave medbay for days. It was also day when crew crew learned that she had a large sweet tooth.Both Garuss and Wrex joked about it.

She looked at gifts once aggain.Goddes she sighed loudly, sat heavily on nearby chair and put her face in hands. It was too much, knowledge that her lovers body was in Cerbeus hands, the looming threat of Reapers, her failure to help Feron and most imprtantly imminet threat that haunted her dreams, The Obserwer. If she don,t catch him he or she will dispse of her sooner or later.

"No" she said and took a deep breath."You fought against enemies more dangerous than The Collector and won. At the very least you must stay alive to help Shepard fight against Reapers" she reassued heself.


She glanced on box once again. She did lovo chocolate.
12323.title: A...food delivery?

123231.content:
Former achelolog stand up, took the box and relocated to one of couches in living room. She opened it, inside there was several rows of very appetizing, but ordinary chocolates.

It's unhealthy to eat sweets that early, but I need to relax. I am already bit late, but since everything checks out I might as well eat a few chocolatesLiara thought to herself. She put open box beside herself, leaned more comfortably, picked single chocolate and examined it. Nothing out of the ordinary.

"I am getting too paranoid" she said and popped chocolate into her mouth."Mmmm" she moaned quietly. It was really good and not too sweet like many treats tended to be. She looked through window and admired rising sun while taking another piece of chocolate.

When was the last time I allowed myself to trully rellax mused Liara and chuckled sadlyProbably on that party before Normady set out again on its last voyage She remembered it well, Garrus and Wrex bickering, hilariously drunk Tali, a drinking contest between Chief Williams and Doctor Chakwas and of course Shepard. Commander was there, smiling, joking and flirting shamelessly with her. Most importantly she was alive and right beside her. Our own makeshift family

Liara shook her head. It was year since Normady's destruction. If Cerberus was truthfull they may be able to bring Shepard back. Galaxy at large needed her, despite Councillor Anderson efforts all other resentatives denies existence of Repairs and refused to do anything about threat.

"Then again since when did they were any help" thought asari and checked time on her omnitool. 8:30 am she need to hurry if she wanted to be in her office along with Nyxeris.

She trusted her assistan, to a degree. Not enough to usually let her arrive before her and possibly plant a bug or hack her terminal and look on her files. It was common sense really. She had enough enemies old and new willing to pay considerable sum for her

She stood up andrelized she is quite full, significantly more than after breakfast. She checked box and realized that she ate over half of its content.

That much!? I should have noticed, I guess i got lost in thought but still... thougt assari but, chose not to dwell on it. She looked in the mirror and at her hands. She notices small smudges of chocolate at her fingers and mouth. She needed to clean herself... but it was such good chocolate.

Before she coud stop herseld she started licking it from ger fingers and mouth. Get a hold of yourself T'Soni she scolded herself and went to clean herself in bathroom.

"I really need to get some decent sleep" she commented as she exited her apartment.While she worked well under pressure constant threat of The Observe and nightmares prevented her from getting any peaceful sleep.

If I don't find him soon my sleep deprivation and nerves will do his job for him

Unknowingly to her chocolates she just have eaten were far from ordinary. While its extra ingredients were anything but harmful they indeed had rather unique effects on asari physiology.

She was also aware of well hidden presence that observed through window entire incident form one of skyscrapers.

It was...

12323112.title: Shadow Broker operative

123231121.content:
In one of the skyscrapers seen from Liara's window was rarher unussual apartment. Besides tipical furniture it was filled with extremly advanced spying equipment. It was being used by Shadow Broker agents to oberve on asari archeologist

Right now it was occupied by one person, Shadow Broker top operative on Illium, The Observer better known as Nyxeris, assistant to none other but Liara T'Soni. Usually she didn't participate in grunt work, but in this case she had to make shure that operation was sucessful while keeping all scraples of information away from Benezia's daughter.

Despite sophisticated equipment, due to distance and various anti-spy devices used by Liara all she received was decent view of her target's living room and bits of sounds. Fortunately, right now it was enough.

Nyxeris watched as her oficial employer sat on her coutch. After seeing Liara eat first chocolate she turned off devices and smirked sweetly to herself.

"Now the real fun begins" said asari to herself, while opening special secure channel on her omnitool and begin typing:

"First phase of operation "Ocean" proceed as planned, beginning phase two"

"Acknoledged, continue with operatin"

Still smirking Nyxeris shut down her omnitool and began walking to exit, she needed to be punctual after all.

Preparing everything was not easy, finding method of implementing the plan took some time. Liara never ordered food while at work, having prepared meals herself and bringing them for later to her office. That human Constantine provided unexpected opportunity, he simply contacter her one day, asking her "in secret" what kind of gifts Liara liked. Thanks to Shadow Broker database she was able identify objects that good doctor had weakness for. After that only thing left was to fabricate chocolates according to her needs and swapping them with original.

Nyxeris know about device Liara used to scan items, however she had no intention of poisoning or even drugging her fellow asari so it posed no problem. Substances inside chocolates had other, non threatening but still quite interesting abilities. They speed up digestion process while making it more effective, reduced feeling of fullness, make stomach more prone to stretching. Most importantly they induce feeling of hunger.

Dose of drugs which Liara ate along with chocolates should give her opportunity feed archaeologist more of them as well as introduce more aggressive and invasive substances.

Reason behind all of it? Miss T'Soni posed a threat to Broker's operations as well as to Nyxeris own safety, not to mention her past meddlings with his operations. Of course she could kill Liara, despite her paranoia and various security measures it was doable provided she caught her off guard. However it would not be without consequences, Normandy's remaining crew while estranged would most likely come looking for ones responsible , which would cause further problems. At the same time she was valuable resource having contacts and connections Nyxeris had no chance of acquiring by herself but having acces to as Liara's assistant.

What's better way to increase Liara trust in her while limiting her ability to perform certain activities and not taking away too much of her mental process which made her such useful asset in the first place? Dutifully taking care of her and other task she shifted to her assistant as her weight and hunger keep increasing. Perhaps with slight help from compliance drugs in later phases.

Plus thought The Observer to herself as she drove to work turning T'Soni into fatty is reward in itself

Nyxeris enjoyed fear and power she had over Liara as The Observer. Manipulating her to gain weight and becoming dependent on her only added to that feeling.
123231121.title: Nyxeris, The Observer

1232311211.content:
--Liara's office--


Despite surprise this morning, today so far was quite normal for Liara. She even managed to arrive just before her assistant. Unfortunately Nyxeris, besides a few clues and new suspects names, have not been successful in finding anything that could lead her to The Observer or Shadow Broker, still it was better than nothing. As much as she would like to go through data concerning her personal goals she had more "routine" work to do.

I am digging through lies and useless data, connecting scaps of information to see a bigger picture mused young asari Not too different from my work as an archaeologist. Then again menial workers were used to help remove rubble not to shake somebody for information. Fewer death threats and bodies were underground not in the closed while still warm Despite Liara's extreme proficiency she didn't enjoy her current profession. However she needed to locate Shadow Broker and avenge Ferron, not to mention her position as information broker allowed her to gain contacts and influence that may be later helpful against the Reapers.

Liara was in the middle of searching through information about young, populist turian politician on Citadel looking for any useful intel when she heard a low growl that snapped her back to reality. For some time she was aware of the hollow feeling in her middle but deep in thoughts she paid it no mind. Former archaeologist looked and realized the cause of both unexpected interruption and uncomfortable feeling - her stomach. She was positively starving.

What? Between breakfast and half box of chocolates I shouldn't be that hungry thought confused asari and checked time, it was 01 p.m.. Well...it is time for lunch and last few days have been quite draining Around that hour she and Nyxeris took a break. Her assistant usually went to one of the buffets just outside the trade district while she stayed at the office eating meal she prepared earlier at home, looking briefly over any new informations on Shadow Broker and The Observer.

She was about to open a hidden fridge where meals were kept when she realized one more thing. Yesterday she no time to prepare the meal and in the morning, in hurry, she didn't take anything else.

"Goddess, I could use real vacation" sighed heavily Liara, winced and rubbed her stomach trying to soothe hunger pangs. She, of course, had stashed nutrition bars, even military rations, however she preferred not to open supply unless there was an emergency. Not to mention taste, even thought Liara have eaten even worse meals both as an archaeologist and when traveling with Shepard it was not something she enjoyed. It would not lift her spirits to eat a dry ration while drinking water.

Let's think what are my options? she thought to herself as she heard yet another grumble from her stomach. She could ride back home and make herself something. However ride there and back would cost her a lot of time, especially since a rush hour just started.

Two, most buffets and restaurants provided takeaway. She could ask Nyxeris to buy something for her. She would not have to waste time in the car or even preparing meal. The definitive downside of that plan was the lack of her usual inspection of meal safety.

Lastly she would content herself with what she has had in her office. Young doctor shivered at the thought.
Texture of cardboard or sand and that terrible bland taste winced asari at the memory.


What to do?
1232311211.title: Ordinary day at work

choices: She has another guest in the meantime
12323112111.content:
I cannot have my fear of The Observer prevent me to function properly, besides Nyxeris is far from careless reasoned Liara.

It's true. While her assistant didn't quite share her caution she was aware of the danger involved in their activities. From time to time she reviewed actions of her subordinate to see if she took the necessary precautions and she never disappointed. While less vigilant than asari doctor she was careful and thorough.

Then again she doesn't have the same target on her back as I do thought Liara and pressed a button to call for her assistant.

"Nyxeris could I see you here for a moment" she called over the intercom

"Of course" came the reply. After a short moment door opened and her assistant entered.

"Is something the matter ma'am?" asked Nyxeris.

"No, nothing serious at least" replied Liara with a slight, reassuring smile " I simply forgot to take my lunch from home, tell me could you bring buy me something from the place where you eat while I look over data you brought me earlier. I know that is hardly part of duties but I would be grateful if you do. Of course, I will fully compensate you for the meal"

"Of course ma'am, I am happy to help replied asari with a smile.

"But please be even more cautious than usual, maybe I am needlessly worried but I rather avoid being poisoned by one of Broker's agents"

"Of course Liara" confirmed assistant with with small smile and a slight nod "I infrequently change locals and it is always restaurant with open buffet. It's hard to poison a specific person without drawing attention when everybody eats from one container. Also, I scan meals with my omnitool just to be sure."

"Forgive me, I do not wish to question your competence, but I prefer to play it safe" reassured the information broker, trying to hide another growl from her stomach.

"Than you, that will be all for now" she dismissed her assistant who nodded and exited her office.

After doors closed she relaxed a little and rubbed her belly trying to soothe it.

I hope Nyxeris will return shortly because I doubt I will be able to focus properly like that she thought with a grimace and opend file containig all data collected about Shadow broker and The Observer. She needed sort out new deta.

She was unaware of the fact that her assistant did hear her stomach or wide smile that formed on Nyxeris face at the same moment.
12323112111.title: Ask Nyxeris for help

choices: Liara and Nyxeris have a talk
123231121111.content:
01:50 p.m. showed the clock on Liara's terminal. Young asari winced and shook her head, trying to focus. Despite what she had planned she was unable to do almost any work.To blame was her hunger. Feeling in her stomach had gone from uncomfortable to almost unbearable.

That's what I get for allowing myself to get distracted she thought to herself and rubbed her eyes. The last time she was that hungry was when Normandy's crew rescued her from a prothean security device she accidentally triggered.

I almost fainted after meeting with rest of the crew, I guess those last three days affected me more than I thought during her latest erratic search for Shadow Broker hardly allowed for frequent and full meals combining that with 40 hours without sleep and it's no wonder her body was exhausted.
She was about to take a sip of water when she heard the sound of intercom. She clicked on the button and asked
"Yes?"

"Ma'am I am back, may I come in?" was replayed. A quick look at cameras and scans showed that it was indeed Nyxeris.

"I am unlockig the door" said Liara and did just that. At the sight of her assistant her eyes widened slightly. Nyxeris appeared to be a little out of breath, most likely because of two large plastic bags she was holding and that shocked Liara.

"I was unsure what you would like ma'am so I took a few different things" huffed her employer and set both containers on her desk.

"A few?" asked Liara forcing herself to smile.

Nyxeris blushed faintly "I realized just how much, a little too late" she replied apologeticall, then straighten up and added with a confident voice "I took all necessary precautions, all dishes were tested and scanned. No harmful elements or effects were found"

"Thank you Nyxeris I trust your expertise, now before you return to your duties check for any new informations about Joram Talid"

"Of course ma'am, enjoy your meal" her assistant nodded with a smile, turned around and left her with two bags full of food.
Liara began removing boxes from bags. While asari had quite large energy requirements despite their size, she was still looking at a meal for about two or three members of her species.

That's hardly a problem, I am starving and all leftovers I can take with me to home thought former archeologist when opening the first box. It was fried fish, one native to Thessia and surrendered by boiled vegetables. She cut pice and tasted.

Hmm... delicious said young asari and began to eat rest. Next box, two slices of pizza, that hardly surprised her, human cuisine was a big hit among asari, especially younger ones. She, herself was quite familiar and found of it thanks to her time aboard Normandy.

She began devouring dish after dish like an automat, steadily and carefully but fast and only with small breaks for Thessian tea. Fried octopus, those little sausages Liara often eaten as a child, crepes filled with meat and other examples of asari and human cuisine. Her face was being smeared by drops of sauces and juices, but she paid it no mind as she kept eating with a blissful expression. Everything was delicious, much better than what she prepared for herself. A relatively small size of portions was misleading, making young doctor think that she ate less than in reality. Liara didn't register when she started to be full nor when her stomach began to swell and stretch. Her hunger and excellent taste of dishes suppressed those feelings.

After 20 minutes feast came to an end. She put the last empty container back to the bag and wiped her face with napkins. Goddess she thought as she leaned back in her chair and began rubbing her belly with content I had been starving, but still I cannot believe that I managed to eat that much. Liara looked at her stomach and frowned slightly, her skin-tight dress did little to hide her food baby. It's extraordinary situation, I'll do some shopping and prepare meals for the following days she promised to herself without enthusiasm. She felt good, probably the best she had been in weeks. The former archaeologist was still very aware of her troubles and enemies, but they felt more distant at the moment. She felt almost...optimistic.

I guess that are advantages of a full meal prepared by actual cooks mused Liara. While she could cook she was no chef and limited time she had for that activity didn't allow for too complicated dishes. The meal she just ate, was by no elaborate but still leagues above what she usually ate and was much fresher. Another inconvenience caused by The Observer

She signed, cleaned remains of food and hid bags to dispose of them later. Then she reluctantly returned to her abandoned work on turian politician. She had much to do.

***


Nyxeris closed feed on her terminal and saved entire footage to secure external drive, corners of her lips moved up just so slightly. Despite giving no outward signs she was extremely pleased. She just witnessed great Liara T'Soni make glutton of herself. Then again it's nothing compared to what I have planned for her thought asari smugly to herself. Her superior's entire meal was spiked with the same mix as chocolates but with slightly stronger appetite enhancers and few new ingredients that could not pass scanning at Liara's house. A concoction that causes pleasure and feeling of relaxation, nothing strong as she could not raise any suspicions, even though by Illium standards it barely registered as a controlled substance. It was a coupled with a rather unique and short-lasting substance that encouraged mental conditioning, after all the faster Liara start identifying eating with comfort and pleasure the better. The last substance worked in two stages first making the subject gradually tired then sleepy, it was coated in special nanocaspsules which should dissolve in about 3-4 hours after she retired from work. It should force Liara to return home early without allowing her to do much headway in her personal projects while giving her just enough time to do basic chores, eat a hearty meal after a tiring day at work before heading to sleep and prevent her from burning too much of precious calories.

Earlier The Observer entertained the thought of lacing products that Liara bought by herself. However that would cause its own set of problems. Liara, much like herself, changed the provider of ingredients quite often and sometimes she went to buy groceries by herself also in many different stores. Adding something to younger asari food without creating an accident was extremely difficult, not to mention scanner at Liara home prevented her altogether from using certain substances. Another problem was caused by her victim's cooking, it was well balanced and low fat. She wanted Liara became sedentary as quickly as possible, but while using a limited arsenal of drugs merely increasing Benezia's daughter intake of her usual meals wound not fatten her nearly as fast as she woud like. She needed to introduce more "hearty" meals into young asari diet.

While she did buy meals from the same buffet she was in, it was not what she fed to Liara. Everything that just went into younger asari gullet looked and tasted indistinguishable from original, but had one major difference besides earlier mentioned substances. It was incredibly high packed with calories, much more than it was possible to get through normal cooking. It served two purposes, the most obvious one was the fact that it would pile much more fat on Liara than the original. The second reason was less obvious but more important. When doctor T'Soni's body grows accustomed to consuming that many calories in one go it will still crave more even if she was full of lighter food, adding to her hunger and tricking her to further stuff herself. While she could not provide Liara with similar meals every time she eat, at least for now, it would at least strongly encourage her to switch to more calorie-heavy meals.

There is still much work to do, even today thought Nyxeris, she had to maneuver Liara so today would not be single occurrence. She was going over plan when she nearly burst with laughter at the sudden thought Commander Shepard, if she truly is alive, is in for a really big, blue surprise
123231121111.title: Nyxeris returns with with lunch

choices: Liara decided to hear Nyxeris out
1232311211111.content:
--Liara's office, afternoon--


Liara and Nyxeris were sitting together in the information broker office and going over the most important pieces of gathered intel, list of possible new clients as well as checking if they needed to deploy some of the mercenaries working in doctor T'Soni employ to deal with something directly. It was their last activity together, as it was the end of older asari working hours, and unless there was something extremely important or time-sensitive she left for home. There was nothing of that sort that day. As always, the last and most widely discussed was the issue of Shadow Broker and his agent The Observer. Unfortunately today did not bring any significant revelationsThey sorted new data, noted which leads to follow, discarded useless ones, and prepared plans for the following days. Unfortunately they still had nothing concrete besides the register of races provided by Nyxeris. It narrowed the list of suspects significantly, but not nearly enough and the only other concrete information was the fact that The Observer was personally interested in Liara.

There has to be something, some piece of information that The Observer did not erase or a pattern we can use to track him downthought young asari with anger, but mostly with tiredness as she kept looking over data Ultimately, finding him and his employer is a matter of time, but time is something Ferron does not have. She was painfully aware that chances of drell being still alive in Shadow Broker captivity were slim at best, but she could not abandon him.

Liara looked at her assistant sitting opposite, who also searched data on her datapad, and looked at the clock. Nyxeris shift, in theory ended ended 15 minutes ago. She took a deep breath and said:

"Nyxeris I think that will be all for today. Thank you for your help" as much as she would like to keep her assistant longer with her, she could not.From a purely formal standpoint she would rather not being bothered by law enforcement on the ground of worker exploitation. More importantly having her employees angry and disgruntled at her was bad for business and health.

"Ma'am there is something I would like to talk about before I go," said Nyxeris while rubbing her hands nervously and added "I may be overstepping my bounds, but it is important"

Liara tensed, that was most unusual, her assistant was almost always strictly professional, save perhaps for a few occasional jokes in private, and she never talked on personal matters. In one moment she was prepared for a direct fight, betrayal, and air attack. She reminded herself where she hid weapons and had her biotics were ready for action. On the outside none of that was noticeable, she gave her assistant a questioning look and leaned slightly on a chair, all while maintaining perfect, professional composure.

"If you consider something to be important then it is. Please, speak freely" responded prothean expert.

Nyxeris a took deep breath and said:
"Ma'am with all due respect you cannot continue like that"

Well, that's a bold statement
"What, exactly do you mean Nyxeris?" asked Liara with a steady but firm voice.

"Your yesterday's bout of constant research was not the first, but the longest one, that's not healthy or even productive. While I only guess I am sure that even under "normal" circumstances you don't get nearly enough sleep. You maintain perfect composure with our clients and contacts, but I have worked with people suffering from sleep deprivation, I recognize certain signs" said older asari, took a deep breath and continued "I also saw enough of your usual meals, they are bare minimum not to mention they are rather bland and repetitive. You are in constant tension, even more so than other people of our profession"

Nyxeris took few calming breaths, lowered her head a little and added:
"I worry Liara. All of this is bad for you, if it continues you will burn yourself out. You need to relax a little, rest"

Well it's not quite what I expected thought to herself Liara after hearing her employe explanation. SShe must have been slipping if Nyxeris was noticed her bad habits, then again her observation skills were one of the reasons she hired her in the first place. As much as she would like follow that advice there were certain obstacles.

"It is an occupational risk, besides you are aware of my goals as well as that The Observer is constantly monitoring us. I cannot allow myself to relax back, because I would risk being caught off guard. I do not have too much free time anyway"

Nyxeris shook her head. "Ma'am if you continue like that you will be too tired to remain vigilant or work effectively. The Observer is monitoring all persons of interest on Illium. I agree that he pays special attention to you, but considering circumstances it is nothing unexpected. Between being the sole heir to house T'Soni and your...involvement with Commander Shepard, he would focus on you even if you weren't actively trying to hunt down his employer" reasoned calmly older asari while looking Liara directly in the eyes. "Besides you saw data, those fragments of orders and reports that we managed to acquire. Both Shadow Broker and The Observer stress that you need to remain being observed without being harmed in any way. All other sources seem to confirm it"

"Yes I am aware, however, please remember that my ultimate goal is Shadow Broker himself. We cannot count to him to continue with this policy as we progress" responded Liara with a slight shake of her head.

Nyxeris nodded "When he decides he is being threatened he will respond, but this is to our advantage. I saw how you work, during the short time we work together you managed to learn about Broker than I thought possible in such a short period. I work in this area for some time already. I know of people that also hunted him, but to get that close as we are now they spend decades even with more resources. Shadow Broker knows this as well, he can not suspect us to become a threat that soon" reasoned further her assistant. She, once again had a point, but it was not enough.

"Then you also know they got overconfident and were disposed of. We cannot allow ourselves to repeat their mistakes by growing lax" pointed out Liara, still not convinced.

"That brings me to another matter" replied Nyxeris undeterred. "While I do not know all resources available to you, I am certain we currently do not have the means to directly confront Shadow Broker. We may be able to deal with The Observer and his direct subordinates here on Illium. But even if we were to find Broker's location today we don't have influence or resources to deal with him".
